title: Backends: SB vs CRP
text: vignette compares two mixture backends: SB: Stick-breaking (finite truncation), controlled components CRP: Chinese Restaurant Process (cluster allocations)

title: Backends: SB vs CRP
code: library(DPmixGPD)  n <- 90 y <- abs(rnorm(n)) + 0.2

title: Backends: SB vs CRP
code: bundle_sb <- build_nimble_bundle(   y = y,   backend = "sb",   kernel = "normal",   GPD = TRUE,   components = 6,   mcmc = mcmc )  fit_sb <- run_mcmc_bundle_manual(bundle_sb, show_progress = FALSE) #> [MCMC] Creating NIMBLE model... #> [MCMC] NIMBLE model created successfully. #> [MCMC] Configuring MCMC... #> ===== Monitors ===== #> thin = 1: alpha, mean, sd, tail_scale, tail_shape, threshold, w, z #> ===== Samplers ===== #> RW sampler (21) #>   - alpha #>   - mean[]  (6 elements) #>   - sd[]  (6 elements) #>   - threshold #>   - tail_scale #>   - tail_shape #>   - v[]  (5 elements) #> categorical sampler (90) #>   - z[]  (90 elements) #> [MCMC] MCMC configured. #> [MCMC] Building MCMC object... #> [MCMC] MCMC object built. #> [MCMC] Attempting NIMBLE compilation (this may take a minute)... #> [MCMC] Compiling model... #> [MCMC] Compiling MCMC sampler... #> [MCMC] Compilation successful. #> [MCMC] MCMC execution complete. title: Backends: SB vs CRP
text: Posterior Mean Comparison: SB vs CRP
code: mean_sb <- predict(fit_sb, type = "mean", cred.level = 0.90, interval = "credible")$fit mean_crp <- predict(fit_crp, type = "mean", cred.level = 0.90, interval = "credible")$fit  comparison_df <- data.frame(   Backend = c("SB", "CRP"),   Estimate = c(mean_sb$estimate[1], mean_crp$estimate[1]),   Lower = c(mean_sb$lower[1], mean_crp$lower[1]),   Upper = c(mean_sb$upper[1], mean_crp$upper[1]) )  kable(comparison_df, digits = 3, align = "c",       caption = "Posterior Mean Comparison: SB vs CRP") %>%   kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"),                 full_width = FALSE, position = "center")

title: Causal Workflow
text: vignette demonstrates causal inference workflow using build_causal_bundle() run_mcmc_causal(). package computes distributional treatment effects: Average Treatment Effect (ATE): E[Y(1)−Y(0)∣X]E[Y(1) - Y(0) \mid X] Quantile Treatment Effect (QTE): QY(1)(τ)−QY(0)(τ)Q_{Y(1)}(\tau) - Q_{Y(0)}(\tau)

title: Causal Workflow
code: library(DPmixGPD)  n <- 80 X <- data.frame(x = rnorm(n)) T_ind <- rbinom(n, 1, plogis(0.2 + 0.5 * X$x)) y0 <- 0.5 + 0.7 * X$x + abs(rnorm(n)) + 0.1 te <- 0.4 + 0.6 * (X$x > 0) y1 <- y0 + te y <- ifelse(T_ind == 1, y1, y0)

title: Causal Workflow
text: build_causal_bundle() function creates unified structure containing: Propensity score (PS) model (optional) Control arm outcome model Treated arm outcome model
code: bundle <- build_causal_bundle(   y = y,   X = X,   T = T_ind,   backend = "sb",   kernel = "normal",   GPD = TRUE,   components = 6,   PS = "logit",   design = "observational",   mcmc_outcome = mcmc,   mcmc_ps = mcmc )  bundle #> DPmixGPD causal bundle #> PS model: Bayesian logit (T | X)  #> Outcome (treated): backend = sb | kernel = normal  #> Outcome (control): backend = sb | kernel = normal  #> GPD tail (treated/control): TRUE / TRUE  #> components (treated/control): 6 / 6  #> Outcome PS included: TRUE  #> epsilon (treated/control): 0.025 / 0.025  #> n (control) = 38 | n (treated) = 42

title: Causal Workflow
code: fit <- run_mcmc_causal(bundle, show_progress = FALSE) #> ===== Monitors ===== #> thin = 1: beta #> ===== Samplers ===== #> RW sampler (2) #>   - beta[]  (2 elements) #> [MCMC] Creating NIMBLE model... #> [MCMC] NIMBLE model created successfully. #> [MCMC] Configuring MCMC... #> ===== Monitors ===== #> thin = 1: alpha, beta_mean, beta_ps_mean, beta_tail_scale, beta_threshold, sd, sdlog_u, tail_shape, threshold, w, z #> ===== Samplers ===== #> RW sampler (65) #>   - alpha #>   - sd[]  (6 elements) #>   - beta_mean[]  (6 elements) #>   - beta_ps_mean[]  (6 elements) #>   - sdlog_u #>   - beta_tail_scale[]  (1 element) #>   - tail_shape #>   - v[]  (5 elements) #>   - threshold[]  (38 elements) #> conjugate sampler (1) #>   - beta_threshold[]  (1 element) #> categorical sampler (38) #>   - z[]  (38 elements) #> [MCMC] MCMC configured. #> [MCMC] Building MCMC object... #> [MCMC] MCMC object built. #> [MCMC] Attempting NIMBLE compilation (this may take a minute)... #> [MCMC] Compiling model... #> [MCMC] Compiling MCMC sampler... #> [MCMC] Compilation successful. #> [MCMC] MCMC execution complete. Processing results... #> [MCMC] Creating NIMBLE model... #> [MCMC] NIMBLE model created successfully. #> [MCMC] Configuring MCMC... #> ===== Monitors ===== #> thin = 1: alpha, beta_mean, beta_ps_mean, beta_tail_scale, beta_threshold, sd, sdlog_u, tail_shape, threshold, w, z #> ===== Samplers ===== #> RW sampler (69) #>   - alpha #>   - sd[]  (6 elements) #>   - beta_mean[]  (6 elements) #>   - beta_ps_mean[]  (6 elements) #>   - sdlog_u #>   - beta_tail_scale[]  (1 element) #>   - tail_shape #>   - v[]  (5 elements) #>   - threshold[]  (42 elements) #> conjugate sampler (1) #>   - beta_threshold[]  (1 element) #> categorical sampler (42) #>   - z[]  (42 elements) #> [MCMC] MCMC configured. #> [MCMC] Building MCMC object... #> [MCMC] MCMC object built. #> [MCMC] Attempting NIMBLE compilation (this may take a minute)... #> [MCMC] Compiling model... #> [MCMC] Compiling MCMC sampler... #> [MCMC] Compilation successful. #> [MCMC] MCMC execution complete. Processing results... fit #> DPmixGPD causal fit #> PS model: Bayesian logit (T | X)  #> Outcome (treated): backend = sb | kernel = normal  #> Outcome (control): backend = sb | kernel = normal  #> GPD tail (treated/control): TRUE / TRUE

title: Causal Workflow
code: ate_result <- ate(fit, interval = "hpd", nsim_mean = 100) print(ate_result) #> ATE (Average Treatment Effect) #>   Prediction points: 80 #>   Conditional (covariates): YES #>   Propensity score used: YES #>   PS scale: logit #>   Posterior mean draws: 100 #>   Credible interval: hpd #>  #> ATE estimates (treated - control): #>  id estimate  lower upper #>   1   -0.616 -3.028 1.903 #>   2    0.124 -0.850 0.848 #>   3   -0.859 -3.253 2.892 #>   4    0.712 -0.690 1.884 #>   5    0.196 -0.702 1.176 #>   6   -0.869 -3.536 2.671 #> ... (74 more rows)  X_new <- data.frame(x = seq(min(X$x), max(X$x), length.out = 20)) ate_grid <- ate(fit, newdata = X_new, interval = "hpd", nsim_mean = 100, level = 0.90)  print(ate_grid) #> ATE (Average Treatment Effect) #>   Prediction points: 20 #>   Conditional (covariates): YES #>   Propensity score used: YES #>   PS scale: logit #>   Posterior mean draws: 100 #>   Credible interval: hpd #>  #> ATE estimates (treated - control): #>  id estimate  lower upper #>   1   -2.840 -9.079 5.400 #>   2   -2.372 -7.811 5.000 #>   3   -2.043 -7.980 3.055 #>   4   -1.738 -6.583 3.628 #>   5   -1.397 -4.939 3.397 #>   6   -1.029 -4.374 2.133 #> ... (14 more rows) summary(ate_grid) #> ATE Summary #> ==================================================  #> Prediction points: 20 #> Conditional: YES | PS used: YES #> Posterior mean draws: 100 #> Interval: hpd #>  #> Model specification: #>   Backend (trt/con): sb / sb #>   Kernel (trt/con): normal / normal #>   GPD tail (trt/con): YES / YES #>  #> ATE statistics: #>   Mean: -0.347 | Median: 0.053 #>   Range: [-2.84, 0.947] #>   SD: 1.18 #>  #> Credible interval width: #>   Mean: 4.856 | Median: 2.907 #>   Range: [1.233, 14.48]

title: Causal Workflow
text: plot() method ATE objects supports multiple visualization types: type = "" (default): Returns list trt_control treatment_effect plots type = "effect": Treatment effect curve credible intervals type = "arms": Treated vs control mean outcomes
code: ate_plots <- plot(ate_grid) ate_plots$treatment_effect ate_plots$trt_control

title: Causal Workflow
code: probs <- c(0.1, 0.5, 0.9) qte_result <- qte(fit, probs = probs, interval = "hpd") print(qte_result) #> QTE (Quantile Treatment Effect) #>   Prediction points: 80 #>   Quantile grid: 0.1, 0.5, 0.9 #>   Conditional (covariates): YES #>   Propensity score used: YES #>   PS scale: logit #>   Credible interval: hpd #>  #> QTE estimates (treated - control): #>  index id estimate   lower upper #>    0.1  1   -3.523  -8.570 2.117 #>    0.1  2   -2.370  -6.542 2.635 #>    0.1  3   -3.822 -10.235 1.651 #>    0.1  4   -0.314  -6.040 4.471 #>    0.1  5   -2.167  -6.590 3.054 #>    0.1  6   -3.800 -10.179 1.666 #> ... (234 more rows)  qte_grid <- qte(fit, probs = probs, newdata = X_new, interval = "hpd") print(qte_grid) #> QTE (Quantile Treatment Effect) #>   Prediction points: 20 #>   Quantile grid: 0.1, 0.5, 0.9 #>   Conditional (covariates): YES #>   Propensity score used: YES #>   PS scale: logit #>   Credible interval: hpd #>  #> QTE estimates (treated - control): #>  index id estimate   lower upper #>    0.1  1   -5.807 -15.822 3.767 #>    0.1  2   -5.455 -14.665 3.515 #>    0.1  3   -5.103 -14.381 2.303 #>    0.1  4   -4.753 -13.040 1.945 #>    0.1  5   -4.404 -12.452 1.399 #>    0.1  6   -4.056 -10.841 1.757 #> ... (54 more rows) summary(qte_grid) #> QTE Summary #> ==================================================  #> Prediction points: 20 | Quantiles: 3 #> Quantile grid: 0.1, 0.5, 0.9 #> Conditional: YES | PS used: YES #> Interval: hpd #>  #> Model specification: #>   Backend (trt/con): sb / sb #>   Kernel (trt/con): normal / normal #>   GPD tail (trt/con): YES / YES #>  #> QTE by quantile: #>  quantile mean_qte median_qte min_qte max_qte sd_qte #>       0.1   -2.514     -2.499  -5.807   0.507  2.024 #>       0.5   -0.617     -0.021  -3.030   0.666  1.180 #>       0.9    0.113      0.021  -0.585   0.950  0.425 #>  #> Credible interval width: #>   Mean: 7.19 | Median: 7.234 #>   Range: [0.336, 19.589]

title: Conditional Models
text: vignette demonstrates fitting conditional model covariates generating predictions grid new covariate values.

title: Conditional Models
code: library(DPmixGPD)  n <- 120 x <- rnorm(n) X <- data.frame(x = x) y <- 0.5 + 0.8 * x + abs(rnorm(n)) + 0.1

title: Conditional Models
code: bundle <- build_nimble_bundle(   y = y,   X = X,   backend = "sb",   kernel = "normal",   GPD = TRUE,   components = 6,   mcmc = mcmc )  fit <- run_mcmc_bundle_manual(bundle, show_progress = FALSE) #> [MCMC] Creating NIMBLE model... #> [MCMC] NIMBLE model created successfully. #> [MCMC] Configuring MCMC... #> ===== Monitors ===== #> thin = 1: alpha, beta_mean, beta_tail_scale, beta_threshold, sd, sdlog_u, tail_shape, threshold, w, z #> ===== Samplers ===== #> RW sampler (141) #>   - alpha #>   - sd[]  (6 elements) #>   - beta_mean[]  (6 elements) #>   - sdlog_u #>   - beta_tail_scale[]  (1 element) #>   - tail_shape #>   - v[]  (5 elements) #>   - threshold[]  (120 elements) #> conjugate sampler (1) #>   - beta_threshold[]  (1 element) #> categorical sampler (120) #>   - z[]  (120 elements) #> [MCMC] MCMC configured. #> [MCMC] Building MCMC object... #> [MCMC] MCMC object built. #> [MCMC] Attempting NIMBLE compilation (this may take a minute)... #> [MCMC] Compiling model... #> [MCMC] Compiling MCMC sampler... #> [MCMC] Compilation successful. #> [MCMC] MCMC execution complete. Processing results... fit #> MixGPD fit | backend: Stick-Breaking Process | kernel: Normal Distribution | GPD tail: TRUE #> n = 120 | components = 6 | epsilon = 0.025 #> MCMC: niter=600, nburnin=150, thin=2, nchains=1  #> Fit #> Use summary() for posterior summaries; plot() for diagnostics; predict() for predictions.

title: Conditional Models
code: q_levels <- c(0.1, 0.5, 0.9) q_fits <- lapply(q_levels, function(tau) {   predict(fit, x = new_X, type = "quantile", index = tau, cred.level = 0.90, interval = "credible")$fit })  q_df <- do.call(rbind, Map(function(tau, df) {   data.frame(x = new_X$x, tau = tau, estimate = df$estimate, lower = df$lower, upper = df$upper) }, q_levels, q_fits))  head(q_df) #>           x tau  estimate      lower     upper #> 1 -2.214700 0.1 -7.908704 -11.916578 -5.107839 #> 2 -2.022353 0.1 -7.543598 -11.215605 -4.882099 #> 3 -1.830007 0.1 -7.178493 -10.675358 -4.656360 #> 4 -1.637660 0.1 -6.813387 -10.011870 -4.430620 #> 5 -1.445314 0.1 -6.448281  -9.362249 -4.204881 #> 6 -1.252967 0.1 -6.083175  -8.805185 -3.979141 ggplot(q_df, aes(x = x, y = estimate, color = factor(tau))) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = lower, ymax = upper, fill = factor(tau)), alpha = 0.2, color = NA) +   labs(x = "x", y = "Predicted Quantile", color = "Quantile", fill = "Quantile",        title = "Conditional Quantile Curves") +   theme_minimal() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

title: Available Distributions
text: DPmixGPD supports multiple bulk kernels mixture components can optionally splice Generalized Pareto Distribution (GPD) tail beyond threshold.

title: Available Distributions

code: kernel_df <- data.frame(   Kernel = c("normal", "gamma", "lognormal", "cauchy", "laplace", "invgauss", "amoroso"),   Parameters = c(     "mean, sd",     "shape, rate",     "meanlog, sdlog",     "location, scale",     "location, scale",     "mean, shape",     "location, scale, shape"   ) )  kable(kernel_df, align = "c", caption = "Available Bulk Kernels") %>%   kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"),                 full_width = FALSE, position = "center")

title: DPmixGPD: Quick Start
text: vignette provides end--end introduction DPmixGPD workflow: Build model specification Run MCMC sampling Extract fitted values predictions

title: DPmixGPD: Quick Start
text: DPmixGPD fits flexible mixture models bulk distribution can splice Generalized Pareto Distribution (GPD) tail beyond threshold. approach appropriate : center data well described single parametric family extreme right tail requires principled extrapolation

title: DPmixGPD: Quick Start
code: library(DPmixGPD)  n <- 80 y <- abs(rnorm(n)) + 0.15  bundle <- build_nimble_bundle(   y = y,   backend = "sb",   kernel = "normal",   GPD = TRUE,   components = 6,   mcmc = mcmc )  fit <- run_mcmc_bundle_manual(bundle, show_progress = FALSE) #> [MCMC] Creating NIMBLE model... #> [MCMC] NIMBLE model created successfully. #> [MCMC] Configuring MCMC... #> ===== Monitors ===== #> thin = 1: alpha, mean, sd, tail_scale, tail_shape, threshold, w, z #> ===== Samplers ===== #> RW sampler (21) #>   - alpha #>   - mean[]  (6 elements) #>   - sd[]  (6 elements) #>   - threshold #>   - tail_scale #>   - tail_shape #>   - v[]  (5 elements) #> categorical sampler (80) #>   - z[]  (80 elements) #> [MCMC] MCMC configured. #> [MCMC] Building MCMC object... #> [MCMC] MCMC object built. #> [MCMC] Attempting NIMBLE compilation (this may take a minute)... #> [MCMC] Compiling model... #> [MCMC] Compiling MCMC sampler... #> [MCMC] Compilation successful. #> [MCMC] MCMC execution complete. Processing results... fit #> MixGPD fit | backend: Stick-Breaking Process | kernel: Normal Distribution | GPD tail: TRUE #> n = 80 | components = 6 | epsilon = 0.025 #> MCMC: niter=400, nburnin=100, thin=2, nchains=1  #> Fit #> Use summary() for posterior summaries; plot() for diagnostics; predict() for predictions.

title: Kernels Overview
text: DPmixGPD supports multiple mixture kernel families flexible density estimation. kernel different properties suited various data types modeling scenarios.

title: Kernels Overview
text: kernels location scale parameters, making suitable real-valued data: Normal - Gaussian kernel mean sd parameters. Best symmetric, bell-shaped distributions. Cauchy - Heavy-tailed kernel location scale parameters. Robust outliers support GPD tail. Laplace - Double exponential kernel location scale parameters. heavier tails Normal.

title: Kernels Overview
text: kernels defined positive values , ideal positive data like durations, sizes, amounts: Lognormal - Log-transformed Normal meanlog sdlog parameters. Good right-skewed positive data. Gamma - Flexible positive kernel shape scale parameters. Can model various skewness levels. Inverse Gaussian - Alternative positive kernel mean shape parameters. Often used waiting times.

title: Kernels Overview
text: Amoroso - Four-parameter generalization loc, scale, shape1, shape2. Includes Gamma, Weibull, distributions special cases.

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: vignette documents probability distributions exported DPmixGPD corresponding d/p/q/r functions used throughout package. continuous random variable YY parameter scalars 𝛉\boldsymbol{\theta}, use standard quartet: Density (PDF): f(y∣𝛉)f(y\mid\boldsymbol{\theta}), implemented d*(). Distribution (CDF): F(y∣𝛉)=Pr(Y≤y)F(y\mid\boldsymbol{\theta})=\Pr(Y\le y), implemented p*(). Quantile: Q(p∣𝛉)=inf{y:F(y∣𝛉)≥p}Q(p\mid\boldsymbol{\theta}) = \inf\{y: F(y\mid\boldsymbol{\theta})\ge p\}, implemented q*(). Random generation: Y=Q(U∣𝛉)Y = Q(U\mid\boldsymbol{\theta}) U∼Unif(0,1)U\sim \text{Unif}(0,1), implemented r*(). d*() functions optionally return log-densities via log = TRUE, .e., logf(y∣𝛉)\log f(y\mid\boldsymbol{\theta}). numerically convenient likelihoods multiply densities add log-densities: log{∏=1nf(yi∣𝛉)}=∑=1nlogf(yi∣𝛉). \log\Big\{\prod_{=1}^n f(y_i\mid\boldsymbol{\theta})\Big\}=\sum_{=1}^n \log f(y_i\mid\boldsymbol{\theta}). p*() functions support lower.tail log.p: Pr(Y>y)=1−F(y),logF(y)orlog{1−F(y)}. \Pr(Y>y)=1-F(y),\qquad \log F(y)\ \text{}\ \log\{1-F(y)\}. lowercase d*(), p*(), q*() functions (e.g., dgammamix, pgammamix) accept vector inputs first argument evaluate elementwise; r*() supports n > 1 (including n = 0). CamelCase versions (e.g., dGammaMix) NIMBLE-compatible scalar functions use model code.
code: w <- c(0.60, 0.40) shape <- c(2.0, 5.0) scale <- c(1.0, 2.0)  # Use lowercase vectorized wrappers for R-side usage dgammamix(seq(0.5, 2.0, length.out = 5), w = w, shape = shape, scale = scale) [1] 0.1819845 0.2190497 0.2155592 0.1936004 0.1654680 qgammamix(c(0.1, 0.5, 0.9), w = w, shape = shape, scale = scale) [1]  0.7309733  3.1258068 12.5500460 rgammamix(5, w = w, shape = shape, scale = scale) [1]  1.1270544  3.4631704 10.4522708  0.5783015  8.9755486

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: Several kernels available finite mixtures. Let fj(⋅∣𝛉j)f_j(\cdot\mid\boldsymbol{\theta}_j) denote jjth component density let 𝐰=(w1,…,wJ)\boldsymbol{w}=(w_1,\ldots,w_J) mixture weights wj≥0w_j\ge 0 ∑j=1Jwj=1\sum_{j=1}^J w_j=1. mixture density CDF f(y)=∑j=1Jwjfj(y∣𝛉j),F(y)=∑j=1JwjFj(y∣𝛉j). f(y)=\sum_{j=1}^J w_j f_j(y\mid\boldsymbol{\theta}_j), \qquad F(y)=\sum_{j=1}^J w_j F_j(y\mid\boldsymbol{\theta}_j). Random generation proceeds sampling latent component index Z∼Categorical(𝐰)Z\sim\text{Categorical}(\boldsymbol{w}), sampling Y∣Z=j∼fj(⋅∣𝛉j)Y\mid Z=j\sim f_j(\cdot\mid\boldsymbol{\theta}_j). Mixture quantiles typically require numerical inversion mixture CDF.

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: Generalized Pareto Distribution (GPD) used tail modeling relative threshold uu. spliced kernels (*Gpd functions), bulk kernel used uu GPD tail attached uu, parameters (u,σ,ξ)(u,\sigma,\xi). code, represented threshold = u, tail_scale = \sigma, tail_shape = \xi (standalone GPD functions use scale shape σ\sigma ξ\xi).

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: standalone GPD functions dGpd, pGpd, qGpd, rGpd implement distribution XXabove threshold uu. x≥ux\ge u, density f(x;u,σ,ξ)=1σ(1+ξx−uσ)−1/ξ−1,σ>0,1+ξx−uσ>0. f(x;u,\sigma,\xi)=\frac{1}{\sigma}\left(1+\xi\frac{x-u}{\sigma}\right)^{-1/\xi-1}, \quad \sigma>0, \quad 1+\xi\frac{x-u}{\sigma}>0. CDF F(x)=1−(1+ξx−uσ)−1/ξ,x≥u, F(x)=1-\left(1+\xi\frac{x-u}{\sigma}\right)^{-1/\xi}, \quad x\ge u,  quantile Q(p)=u+σξ[(1−p)−ξ−1],p∈(0,1),ξ≠0, Q(p)=u+\frac{\sigma}{\xi}\Big[(1-p)^{-\xi}-1\Big],\quad p\(0,1),\ \xi\ne 0,  exponential-limit case obtained ξ→0\xi\0. Parameter mapping (math →\rightarrow code): u→u\tothreshold, σ→\sigma\toscale, ξ→\xi\toshape. Log-densities returned log = TRUE. show DPmixGPD kernels pairs: bulk-GPD-augmented. GPD tail threshold uu uses fGPD(x;u,σ,ξ)=1σ(1+ξx−uσ)−1/ξ−1for x≥u.f_{GPD}(x; u, \sigma, \xi) = \frac{1}{\sigma} \left(1 + \xi \frac{x-u}{\sigma}\right)^{-1/\xi - 1} \ \text{} x \ge u. standalone GPD tail distribution available via dGpd, pGpd, qGpd, rGpd functions.  subsection defines available d/p/q/rd/p/q/r functions, prints example outputs fixed parameters, overlays density curves three parameter sets clear legends. parameter sets reused within bulk GPD variants section.
code: dGpd(1.8, threshold = 1.5, scale = 0.5, shape = 0.2) [1] 1.013262 dGpd(1.8, threshold = 1.5, scale = 0.5, shape = 0.2, log = TRUE) [1] 0.01317507 pGpd(1.8, threshold = 1.5, scale = 0.5, shape = 0.2) [1] 0.4325731 pGpd(1.8, threshold = 1.5, scale = 0.5, shape = 0.2, lower.tail = FALSE) [1] 0.5674269 pGpd(1.8, threshold = 1.5, scale = 0.5, shape = 0.2, log.p = TRUE) [1] -0.8380038 q_vec(qGpd, c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75), threshold = 1.5, scale = 0.5, shape = 0.2) [1] 1.648060 1.871746 2.298770 q_vec(qGpd, c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75), threshold = 1.5, scale = 0.5, shape = 0.2,       lower.tail = FALSE) [1] 2.298770 1.871746 1.648060 q_vec(qGpd, c(log(0.25), log(0.5), log(0.75)), threshold = 1.5, scale = 0.5,       shape = 0.2, log.p = TRUE) [1] 1.648060 1.871746 2.298770 draw_many(rGpd, list(threshold = 1.5, scale = 0.5, shape = 0.2)) [1] 1.750848 2.376390 3.314836 1.622107 2.087044 grid <- seq(-4, 15, length.out = 500) gpd_sets <- list(   list(label = "GPD A", threshold = 1.5, tail_scale = 0.5, tail_shape = 0.2),   list(label = "GPD B", threshold = 2.0, tail_scale = 0.6, tail_shape = 0.15),   list(label = "GPD C", threshold = 1.0, tail_scale = 0.4, tail_shape = 0.25) )  df_gpd <- do.call(rbind, lapply(gpd_sets, function(ps) {   data.frame(x = grid, density = density_curve(grid, dGpd, list(threshold = ps$threshold, scale = ps$tail_scale, shape = ps$tail_shape)), label = ps$label) }))  ggplot(df_gpd, aes(x = x, y = density, color = label)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   labs(title = "Standalone GPD tails", x = "x", y = "density") +   theme_minimal() + theme(legend.position = "top")

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: normal component parameterized μ∈ℝ\mu\\mathbb{R} σ>0\sigma>0: f(y∣μ,σ)=12πσexp(−(y−μ)22σ2). f(y\mid\mu,\sigma)=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi}\sigma}\exp\!\left(-\frac{(y-\mu)^2}{2\sigma^2}\right). finite normal mixture JJ components density f(y)=∑j=1Jwj𝒩(y∣μj,σj2),∑j=1Jwj=1. f(y)=\sum_{j=1}^J w_j\,\mathcal{N}(y\mid\mu_j,\sigma_j^2), \qquad \sum_{j=1}^J w_j=1. Parameter mapping (math →\rightarrow code): μj→\mu_j\tomean[j], σj→\sigma_j\tosd[j], wj→w_j\tow[j].

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: spliced version, bulk distribution normal mixture uu, GPD tail attached uu. tail parameters σ>0\sigma>0 ξ\xi, applied exceedances x−ux-u. Tail mapping (math →\rightarrow code): u→u\tothreshold, σ→\sigma\totail_scale, ξ→\xi\totail_shape.

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
code: grid <- seq(-4, 5, length.out = 400) normal_sets <- list(   list(label = "Mix A", w = c(0.6, 0.3, 0.1), mean = c(-1, 0.5, 2), sd = c(2, 0.6, 1.1)),   list(label = "Mix B", w = c(0.5, 0.3, 0.2), mean = c(-1.2, 0.3, 1.5), sd = c(0.9, 0.7, 1.0)),   list(label = "Mix C", w = c(0.4, 0.35, 0.25), mean = c(-0.5, 2, 2.5), sd = c(0.7, 0.6, 1.2)) )  example <- normal_sets[[1]] dNormMix(0, w = example$w, mean = example$mean, sd = example$sd) [1] 0.2535206 dNormMix(0, w = example$w, mean = example$mean, sd = example$sd, log = TRUE) [1] -1.37231 pNormMix(0, w = example$w, mean = example$mean, sd = example$sd) [1] 0.4790278 pNormMix(0, w = example$w, mean = example$mean, sd = example$sd, lower.tail = FALSE) [1] 0.5209722 pNormMix(0, w = example$w, mean = example$mean, sd = example$sd, log.p = TRUE) [1] -0.7359966 q_vec(qNormMix, c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75), w = example$w, mean = example$mean,       sd = example$sd) [1] -1.42337727  0.08046623  0.94945934 q_vec(qNormMix, c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75), w = example$w, mean = example$mean,       sd = example$sd, lower.tail = FALSE) [1]  0.94945934  0.08046623 -1.42337727 q_vec(qNormMix, c(log(0.25), log(0.5), log(0.75)), w = example$w,       mean = example$mean, sd = example$sd, log.p = TRUE) [1] -1.42337727  0.08046623  0.94945934 draw_many(rNormMix, list(w = example$w, mean = example$mean, sd = example$sd)) [1] -2.2424812 -1.5984302 -1.0898672 -2.7838423  0.9927327 df_norm <- do.call(rbind, lapply(normal_sets, function(ps) {   data.frame(x = grid, density = density_curve(grid, dNormMix, list(w = ps$w, mean = ps$mean, sd = ps$sd)), label = ps$label) }))  ggplot(df_norm, aes(x = x, y = density, color = label)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   labs(title = "Normal mixtures (bulk)", x = "x", y = "density") +   theme_minimal() + theme(legend.position = "top")

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: Spliced density uses mixture x<ux<u attaches fGPDf_{GPD} uu continuity. CDF combines mixture CDF uu GPD exceedance beyond uu; quantiles invert spliced CDF; RNG draws bulk vs tail CDF mass uu.
code: normal_gpd_sets <- list(   list(label = "Mix A", w = c(0.6, 0.4), mean = c(-1, 2), sd = c(0.5, 0.8), threshold = 1.8, tail_scale = 0.4, tail_shape = 0.25),   list(label = "Mix B", w = c(0.5, 0.5), mean = c(0, 1), sd = c(0.6, 0.6), threshold = 1.5, tail_scale = 0.3, tail_shape = 0.2),   list(label = "Mix C", w = c(0.7, 0.3), mean = c(0.5, 2.5), sd = c(0.4, 1.0), threshold = 2.0, tail_scale = 0.5, tail_shape = 0.15) )  example <- normal_gpd_sets[[1]] dNormMixGpd(2, w = example$w, mean = example$mean, sd = example$sd, threshold = example$threshold, tail_scale = example$tail_scale, tail_shape = example$tail_shape) [1] 0.3322395 pNormMixGpd(2, w = example$w, mean = example$mean, sd = example$sd, threshold = example$threshold, tail_scale = example$tail_scale, tail_shape = example$tail_shape) [1] 0.8504922 q_vec(qNormMixGpd, c(0.5, 0.9), w = example$w, mean = example$mean, sd = example$sd, threshold = example$threshold, tail_scale = example$tail_scale, tail_shape = example$tail_shape) [1] -0.5173894  2.1903912 draw_many(rNormMixGpd, example) [1] -0.5405113  2.1568247  0.4085186  1.9550970 -1.7353762 df_norm_gpd <- do.call(rbind, lapply(normal_gpd_sets, function(ps) {   data.frame(x = grid, density = density_curve(grid, dNormMixGpd, list(w = ps$w, mean = ps$mean, sd = ps$sd, threshold = ps$threshold, tail_scale = ps$tail_scale, tail_shape = ps$tail_shape)), label = ps$label) }))  ggplot(df_norm_gpd, aes(x = x, y = density, color = label)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   labs(title = "Normal mixtures with GPD tail (different thresholds)", x = "x", y = "density") +   theme_minimal() + theme(legend.position = "top")

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: gamma component shape α>0\alpha>0 scale β>0\beta>0 density f(y∣α,β)=1Γ(α)βαyα−1exp(−yβ),y>0. f(y\mid \alpha,\beta) = \frac{1}{\Gamma(\alpha)\,\beta^\alpha}\,y^{\alpha-1}\exp\!\left(-\frac{y}{\beta}\right), \qquad y>0. finite gamma mixture JJ components f(y)=∑j=1Jwjfj(y∣αj,βj),wj≥0,∑j=1Jwj=1. f(y)=\sum_{j=1}^J w_j\,f_j(y\mid \alpha_j,\beta_j), \qquad w_j\ge 0,\ \sum_{j=1}^J w_j=1. Parameter mapping (math →\rightarrow code): α→\alpha\toshape, β→\beta\toscale, weights wj→w_j\tow[j]. *MixGpd variant uses splicing idea: gamma mixture uu GPD tail uu. Tail mapping (math →\rightarrow code): u→u\tothreshold, σ→\sigma\totail_scale, ξ→\xi\totail_shape.

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
code: grid <- seq(0, 10, length.out = 400) gamma_sets <- list(   list(label = "Mix A", w = c(0.6, 0.3, 0.1), shape = c(2.0, 5.0, 9.0), scale = c(1.0, 0.6, 0.3)),   list(label = "Mix B", w = c(0.5, 0.3, 0.2), shape = c(1.5, 4.0, 7.0), scale = c(1.2, 0.7, 0.35)),   list(label = "Mix C", w = c(0.4, 0.3, 0.3), shape = c(1.2, 3.5, 6.0), scale = c(1.4, 0.75, 0.4)) ) example <- gamma_sets[[1]]  dens <- do.call(rbind, lapply(gamma_sets, function(s) {   data.frame(     x = grid,     density = dGammaMix(grid, w = s$w, shape = s$shape, scale = s$scale),     label = s$label   ) })) dGammaMix(2, w = example$w, shape = example$shape, scale = example$scale) [1] 0.2952082 dGammaMix(2, w = example$w, shape = example$shape, scale = example$scale, log = TRUE) [1] -1.220074 pGammaMix(2, w = example$w, shape = example$shape, scale = example$scale) [1] 0.452308 pGammaMix(2, w = example$w, shape = example$shape, scale = example$scale, lower.tail = FALSE) [1] 0.547692 pGammaMix(2, w = example$w, shape = example$shape, scale = example$scale, log.p = TRUE) [1] -0.7933919 qGammaMix(0.95, w = example$w, shape = example$shape, scale = example$scale) [1] 5.008484 qGammaMix(0.95, w = example$w, shape = example$shape, scale = example$scale, lower.tail = FALSE) [1] 0.4747765 df_gamma <- do.call(rbind, lapply(gamma_sets, function(ps) {   data.frame(x = grid, density = density_curve(grid, dGammaMix, list(w = ps$w, shape = ps$shape, scale = ps$scale)), label = ps$label) }))  ggplot(df_norm, aes(x = x, y = density, color = label)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   labs(title = "Gamma mixtures (bulk)", x = "x", y = "density") +   theme_minimal() + theme(legend.position = "top")

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: lognormal component defined logY∼𝒩(μ,σ2)\log Y \sim \mathcal{N}(\mu,\sigma^2), .e., f(y∣μ,σ)=1yσ2πexp(−(logy−μ)22σ2),y>0. f(y\mid\mu,\sigma)=\frac{1}{y\,\sigma\sqrt{2\pi}}\exp\!\left(-\frac{(\log y-\mu)^2}{2\sigma^2}\right),\quad y>0. finite lognormal mixture density f(y)=∑j=1JwjLognormal(y∣μj,σj2),y>0. f(y)=\sum_{j=1}^J w_j\,\text{Lognormal}(y\mid\mu_j,\sigma_j^2),\quad y>0. Parameter mapping (math →\rightarrow code): μj→\mu_j\tomeanlog[j], σj→\sigma_j\tosdlog[j], wj→w_j\tow[j].

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: *MixGpd variant uses splicing idea: lognormal mixture uu, GPD tail uu. Tail mapping (math →\rightarrow code): u→u\tothreshold, σ→\sigma\totail_scale, ξ→\xi\totail_shape.

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: Laplace (double-exponential) component location ℓ∈ℝ\ell\\mathbb{R} scale b>0b>0 density f(y∣ℓ,b)=12bexp(−|y−ℓ|b),y∈ℝ. f(y\mid \ell,b)=\frac{1}{2b}\exp\!\left(-\frac{|y-\ell|}{b}\right),\quad y\\mathbb{R}. finite Laplace mixture f(y)=∑j=1JwjLaplace(y∣ℓj,bj). f(y)=\sum_{j=1}^J w_j\,\text{Laplace}(y\mid \ell_j,b_j). Parameter mapping (math →\rightarrow code): ℓj→\ell_j\tolocation[j], bj→b_j\toscale[j], wj→w_j\tow[j].

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: inverse Gaussian component mean μ>0\mu>0 shape λ>0\lambda>0 density f(y∣μ,λ)=(λ2πy3)1/2exp(−λ(y−μ)22μ2y),y>0. f(y\mid \mu,\lambda) = \left(\frac{\lambda}{2\pi y^3}\right)^{1/2} \exp\!\left(-\frac{\lambda (y-\mu)^2}{2\mu^2 y}\right), \quad y>0. finite inverse Gaussian mixture f(y)=∑j=1JwjIG(y∣μj,λj),y>0. f(y)=\sum_{j=1}^J w_j\,\text{IG}(y\mid \mu_j,\lambda_j),\quad y>0. Parameter mapping (math →\rightarrow code): μj→\mu_j\tomean[j], λj→\lambda_j\toshape[j], wj→w_j\tow[j].

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: Amoroso component location ∈ℝa\\mathbb{R}, scale θ≠0\theta\neq 0, shapes α>0\alpha>0, β>0\beta>0 density (y>ay>) f(y∣,θ,α,β)=1Γ(α)|βθ|(y−aθ)αβ−1exp[−(y−aθ)β]. f(y\mid ,\theta,\alpha,\beta) = \frac{1}{\Gamma(\alpha)} \left|\frac{\beta}{\theta}\right| \left(\frac{y-}{\theta}\right)^{\alpha\beta-1} \exp\!\left[-\left(\frac{y-}{\theta}\right)^{\beta}\right]. finite Amoroso mixture f(y)=∑j=1Jwjfj(y∣aj,θj,αj,βj). f(y)=\sum_{j=1}^J w_j\,f_j(y\mid a_j,\theta_j,\alpha_j,\beta_j). Parameter mapping (math →\rightarrow code): aj→a_j\toloc[j], θj→\theta_j\toscale[j], αj→\alpha_j\toshape1[j], βj→\beta_j\toshape2[j], wj→w_j\tow[j].

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: spliced kernel attaches GPD tail uu. Tail mapping (math →\rightarrow code): u→u\tothreshold, σ→\sigma\totail_scale, ξ→\xi\totail_shape.

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: section documents inverse Gaussian density , single component rather mixture: f(y∣μ,λ)=(λ2πy3)1/2exp(−λ(y−μ)22μ2y),y>0. f(y\mid \mu,\lambda) = \left(\frac{\lambda}{2\pi y^3}\right)^{1/2} \exp\!\left(-\frac{\lambda (y-\mu)^2}{2\mu^2 y}\right), \quad y>0. Parameter mapping (math →\rightarrow code): μ→\mu\tomean, λ→\lambda\toshape.

what: Inverse Gaussian base with GPD tail
title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: dInvGaussGpd, pInvGaussGpd, qInvGaussGpd, rInvGaussGpd functions splice base inverse Gaussian uu GPD tail uu. Tail mapping (math →\rightarrow code): u→u\tothreshold, σ→\sigma\totail_scale, ξ→\xi\totail_shape.

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: Cauchy distribution location x0∈ℝx_0\\mathbb{R} scale γ>0\gamma>0 density f(y∣x0,γ)=1πγ[1+(y−x0γ)2],y∈ℝ. f(y\mid x_0,\gamma)=\frac{1}{\pi\gamma\left[1+\left(\frac{y-x_0}{\gamma}\right)^2\right]}, \quad y\\mathbb{R}. finite Cauchy mixture f(y)=∑j=1JwjCauchy(y∣x0j,γj). f(y)=\sum_{j=1}^J w_j\,\text{Cauchy}(y\mid x_{0j},\gamma_j). Parameter mapping (math →\rightarrow code): x0→x_0\tolocation, γ→\gamma\toscale; mixtures x0j→x_{0j}\tolocation[j], γj→\gamma_j\toscale[j], wj→w_j\tow[j].

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: section provides compact reference distributions supported DPmixGPD corresponding user-facing function families. CRP backend uses base (single-kernel) functions, SB backend uses mixture (multi-component) functions. “Type” column indicates whether backend expects scalar parameters (single value per parameter) vectors indexed mixture component.

title: 2. Available Distributions and Related Functions
text: CRP Backend uses base (single-kernel) functions; parameters scalar. SB Backend uses mixture/spliced mixture functions; component-specific parameters typically vectors indexed component jj; mixture weights w required vector. GPD-, SB entries NA mixture-GPD wrapper SB family.

title: 3. Basic Workflow: Model Specification, Bundle, & MCMC
text: DPmixGPD uses direct two-step workflow: Bundle (build_nimble_bundle): Generate NIMBLE code compile sampler. MCMC (run_mcmc_bundle_manual): Execute posterior sampling.

title: 3. Basic Workflow: Model Specification, Bundle, & MCMC
text: Purpose: Generate NIMBLE model code, compile sampler, prepare MCMC execution.

title: 4. Backends, Kernels, and Workflow Map
text: DPmixGPD two orthogonal dials turn building models: CRP: Chinese Restaurant Process representation. SB: stick-breaking truncation fixed number components. Examples: normal, lognormal, gamma, inverse-Gaussian, Laplace, Cauchy, Amoroso, etc. Optionally, can also turn : GPD = TRUE/FALSE splice Generalized Pareto tail beyond threshold.

what: What changes between CRP and SB?
title: 4. Backends, Kernels, and Workflow Map
text: backends target posterior densities. difference representation: CRP learns random number occupied clusters within finite components cap. SB uses finite components cap learns stick-breaking weights. Practical rule thumb: CRP convenient want adaptive complexity still using finite components cap. SB convenient want predictable memory/time easy vectorization.

what: What does the workflow look like?
title: 4. Backends, Kernels, and Workflow Map
text: DPmixGPD uses consistent build -> run -> summarize loop: Build bundle using build_nimble_bundle() (causal builders TE work). Run MCMC using run_mcmc_bundle_manual(). Inspect summarize using print(), summary(), plot(). Predict using predict() (optionally fitted()).
code: # Build bundle <- build_nimble_bundle(   y = rnorm(50),   backend = "crp",   kernel = "normal",   GPD = FALSE,   components = 5,   mcmc = mcmc )  # Run fit <- load_or_fit("v04-backends-and-workflow-fit", run_mcmc_bundle_manual(bundle))  # Summarize print(fit) MixGPD fit | backend: Chinese Restaurant Process | kernel: Normal Distribution | GPD tail: FALSE n = 50 | components = 5 | epsilon = 0.025 MCMC: niter=400, nburnin=100, thin=2, nchains=1  Fit Use summary() for posterior summaries; plot() for diagnostics; predict() for predictions. summary(fit) MixGPD summary | backend: Chinese Restaurant Process | kernel: Normal Distribution | GPD tail: FALSE | epsilon: 0.025 n = 50 | components = 5 Summary Initial components: 5 | Components after truncation: 1  WAIC: 126.441 lppd: -57.216 | pWAIC: 6.004  Summary table   parameter  mean    sd q0.025 q0.500 q0.975     ess  weights[1] 0.962 0.080  0.735  1.000  1.000  23.073       alpha 0.306 0.291  0.005  0.218  1.046  46.898     mean[1] 0.128 0.133 -0.104  0.112  0.404 150.000       sd[1] 1.539 0.422  0.967  1.483  2.602  80.825 plot(fit) === histogram === === density === === traceplot === === running === === compare_partial === === autocorrelation === === geweke === === caterpillar === === pairs ===

title: 6. Unconditional DPmix with CRP Backend
text: Goal: Estimate density univariate outcome yy using nonparametric Dirichlet Process mixture Chinese Restaurant Process backend. Model: yi|G∼∫K(yi;θ)dG(θ)y_i | G \sim \int K(y_i; \theta) dG(\theta) G∼DP(α,G0)G \sim \text{DP}(\alpha, G_0) Backend: CRP truncation max components

title: 6. Unconditional DPmix with CRP Backend
text: CRP Backend: Flexible component allocation, ideal unknown mixture complexity components Parameter: Higher components allows components increases computation Kernel Choice: Gamma suitable positive, skewed data Diagnostics: Check convergence (Rhat, ESS) posterior predictive fit Next: Compare Stick-Breaking (SB) backend vignette 5

title: 7. Unconditional DPmix with Stick-Breaking Backend
text: Purpose: Showcase stick-breaking backend uses fixed number mixture components (components = J) contrast CRP backend v04. vignette fit two bulk kernels (Gamma Cauchy) dataset highlight heavier tails bulk can change fit even without GPD tail.

title: 7. Unconditional DPmix with Stick-Breaking Backend
text: Summary SB Dataset
code: # Load benchmark dataset data("nc_pos200_k3") y_mixed <- nc_pos200_k3$y  df_data <- data.frame(y = y_mixed)  summary_tbl <- tibble(   statistic = c("N", "Mean", "SD", "Min", "Max"),   value = c(length(y_mixed), mean(y_mixed), sd(y_mixed), min(y_mixed), max(y_mixed)) )  p_raw <- ggplot(df_data, aes(x = y)) +   geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)), bins = 30, fill = "darkorange", alpha = 0.7, color = "black") +   geom_density(color = "darkred", linewidth = 1.2) +   labs(title = "Raw Data: Mixed Gamma Distribution", x = "y", y = "Density") +   theme_minimal()  print(p_raw)

title: 7. Unconditional DPmix with Stick-Breaking Backend
text: build stick-breaking mixture explicitly components = 5 room weight decay keeping MCMC runtime manageable vignette. refit SB model Cauchy kernel comparison.

code: bundle_crp_small <- build_nimble_bundle(   y = y_mixed,   kernel = "gamma",   backend = "crp",   components = 5,   GPD = FALSE,   mcmc = mcmc ) fit_crp_small <- load_or_fit("v07-unconditional-DPmix-SB-fit_crp_small", run_mcmc_bundle_manual(bundle_crp_small))  crp_quant <- predict(fit_crp_small, type = "quantile", index = quant_probs, interval = "credible") sb_quant <- predict(fit_sb_gamma, type = "quantile", index = quant_probs, interval = "credible")  bind_rows(   crp_quant$fit %>% mutate(model = "CRP"),   sb_quant$fit %>% mutate(model = "SB") ) %>%   select(any_of(c("model", "index", "estimate", "lower", "upper"))) %>%   mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 3))) %>%   kable(caption = "Quantile Comparison: CRP vs SB", align = "c") %>%   kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE, position = "center") plot(crp_quant) plot(sb_quant)

title: 7. Unconditional DPmix with Stick-Breaking Backend
text: SB Backend: Fixed components keeps label-switching manageable, diagnostic plots via S3 plot() method show weight dynamics. Kernels matter: Gamma vs Cauchy can behave differently shoulders/tails, even without GPD tail. Predictions: Posterior density, posterior-mean quantiles, mean (via predictive sampling) accessible via predict() credible bands. Backend comparison: CRP fit (v04) SB-gamma fit deliver similar central quantiles SB offers control truncation. Next: Explore tail augmentation (GPD = TRUE) CRP backend v06 SB backend v07.

what: Unconditional DPmixGPD: CRP Backend with Tail Augmentation

text: Purpose: Model bulk distribution DP mixture (“kernel” bulk family) let GPD tail capture extreme values beyond threshold. CRP backend handles partitioning toggle GPD = TRUE augment tail.

title: 8. Unconditional DPmixGPD with CRP Backend
text: Summary Tail Dataset
code: # Load data with an obvious tail data("nc_pos_tail200_k4") y_tail <- nc_pos_tail200_k4$y y_tail <- y_tail[is.finite(y_tail) & y_tail > 0]  # Summaries and table summary_tbl <- tibble(   statistic = c("N", "Mean", "SD", "Min", "Max"),   value = c(length(y_tail), mean(y_tail), sd(y_tail), min(y_tail), max(y_tail)) )  df_data <- data.frame(y = y_tail)  ggplot(df_data, aes(x = y)) +   geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)), bins = 40, fill = "steelblue", color = "black", alpha = 0.6) +   geom_density(color = "red", linewidth = 1) +   labs(title = "Raw Data: Mix of Bulk and Tail", x = "y", y = "Density") +   theme_minimal()

title: 8. Unconditional DPmixGPD with CRP Backend
code: thresholds <- quantile(y_tail, c(0.70, 0.75, 0.80, 0.85, 0.90)) u_threshold <- thresholds["80%"]  df_tail <- tibble(   y = y_tail,   region = ifelse(y_tail > u_threshold, "Tail", "Bulk") )  ggplot(df_tail, aes(x = y, fill = region)) +   geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)), bins = 40, alpha = 0.7, color = "black") +   geom_vline(xintercept = u_threshold, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +   scale_fill_manual(values = c("Bulk" = "steelblue", "Tail" = "red")) +   labs(title = "Threshold Partition", x = "y", y = "Density") +   theme_minimal()

title: 8. Unconditional DPmixGPD with CRP Backend
text: demonstrate value tail augmentation, fit bulk-lognormal DP mixture compare posterior summaries InvGauss+GPD fit. Posterior-Mean Quantiles Bulk-vs GPD Models
code: bundle_bulk_only <- build_nimble_bundle(   y = y_tail,   kernel = "lognormal",   backend = "crp",   GPD = FALSE,   components = 5,   mcmc = mcmc ) fit_bulk_only <- load_or_fit("v08-unconditional-DPmixGPD-CRP-fit_bulk_only", run_mcmc_bundle_manual(bundle_bulk_only)) bulk_quantiles <- predict(fit_bulk_only, type = "quantile", index = quantile_probs) tail_quantiles <- predict(fit_gpd, type = "quantile", index = quantile_probs)  compare_tbl <- bind_rows(   bulk_quantiles$fit %>% mutate(model = "Bulk only"),   tail_quantiles$fit %>% mutate(model = "Bulk + GPD") ) %>%   select(any_of(c("model", "index", "estimate", "lwr", "upr", "lower", "upper")))  compare_tbl %>%   mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 3))) %>%   kable(caption = "Posterior-Mean Quantiles for Bulk-Only vs GPD Models", align = "c") %>%   kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE, position = "center") plot(bulk_quantiles) plot(tail_quantiles)

title: 8. Unconditional DPmixGPD with CRP Backend
text: Threshold Sensitivity: 99th Quantile
code: sensitivity_fits <- lapply(thresholds, function(u) {   bundle <- build_nimble_bundle(     y = y_tail,     kernel = "invgauss",     backend = "crp",     GPD = TRUE,     param_specs = list(       gpd = list(         threshold = list(mode = "fixed", value = u)       )     ),     components = 5,     mcmc = mcmc   )   fit <- load_or_fit("v08-unconditional-DPmixGPD-CRP-fit", run_mcmc_bundle_manual(bundle))   list(threshold = u, fit = fit) })  sensitivity_tbl <- bind_rows(lapply(sensitivity_fits, function(entry) {   quant <- predict(entry$fit, type = "quantile", index = 0.99, interval = "credible")   qfit <- as.data.frame(quant$fit)   qfit$threshold <- entry$threshold    # Keep the usual columns if present   keep <- intersect(names(qfit), c("threshold", "index", "estimate", "lower", "upper", "lwr", "upr"))   qfit <- qfit[, keep, drop = FALSE]    # Standardize to one set of CI column names   if (!"lower" %in% names(qfit) && "lwr" %in% names(qfit)) qfit$lower <- qfit$lwr   if (!"upper" %in% names(qfit) && "upr" %in% names(qfit)) qfit$upper <- qfit$upr    # If estimate isn't present for some reason, fall back to the first numeric column   if (!"estimate" %in% names(qfit)) {     num_cols <- names(qfit)[vapply(qfit, is.numeric, logical(1))]     if (length(num_cols) > 0) qfit$estimate <- qfit[[num_cols[1]]]   }    out <- data.frame(     threshold = qfit$threshold[1],     q_99 = qfit$estimate[1],     q_99_lwr = if ("lower" %in% names(qfit)) qfit$lower[1] else NA_real_,     q_99_upr = if ("upper" %in% names(qfit)) qfit$upper[1] else NA_real_   )   tibble::as_tibble(out) }))  sensitivity_tbl %>%   mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ round(.x, 3))) %>%   kable(caption = "Threshold Sensitivity: 99th Quantile", align = "c") %>%   kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE, position = "center")

title: 10. Conditional DPmix with CRP Backend
code: X_new <- expand.grid(   x1 = seq(-2, 2, length.out = 3),   x2 = c(0, 1),   x3 = 0 ) colnames(X_new) <- colnames(X)  y_grid <- seq(-1, 10, length.out = 200) densities_normal <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(X_new)), function(i) {   pred <- predict(fit_cond_normal, x = as.matrix(X_new[i, , drop = FALSE]), y = y_grid, type = "density")   data.frame(     y = pred$fit$y,     density = pred$fit$density,     group = paste0("x1=", round(X_new[i, "x1"], 1), ", x2=", X_new[i, "x2"]),     model = "Normal"   ) })  densities_amoroso <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(X_new)), function(i) {   pred <- predict(fit_cond_amoroso, x = as.matrix(X_new[i, , drop = FALSE]), y = y_grid, type = "density")   data.frame(     y = pred$fit$y,     density = pred$fit$density,     group = paste0("x1=", round(X_new[i, "x1"], 1), ", x2=", X_new[i, "x2"]),     model = "Amoroso (shape1=1)"   ) })  df_dens <- bind_rows(densities_normal, densities_amoroso)  ggplot(df_dens, aes(x = y, y = density, color = group)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   facet_wrap(~ model) +   labs(title = "Conditional Predictive Densities", x = "y", y = "Density") +   theme_minimal() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

code: X_grid <- cbind(   x1 = seq(-2, 2, length.out = 5),   x2 = 0,   x3 = 0 ) colnames(X_grid) <- colnames(X)  quant_probs <- c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75) pred_q_normal <- predict(fit_cond_normal, x = as.matrix(X_grid), type = "quantile", index = quant_probs) pred_q_amoroso <- predict(fit_cond_amoroso, x = as.matrix(X_grid), type = "quantile", index = quant_probs)  quant_df_normal <- pred_q_normal$fit quant_df_normal$x1 <- X_grid[quant_df_normal$id, "x1"] quant_df_normal$model <- "Normal"  quant_df_amoroso <- pred_q_amoroso$fit quant_df_amoroso$x1 <- X_grid[quant_df_amoroso$id, "x1"] quant_df_amoroso$model <- "Amoroso (shape1=1)"  bind_rows(quant_df_normal, quant_df_amoroso) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = x1, y = estimate, color = factor(index), group = index)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   geom_point(size = 2) +   facet_wrap(~ model) +   labs(title = "Conditional Quantiles vs x1 (x2=0)", x = "x1", y = "y", color = "Quantile") +   theme_minimal()

title: 10. Conditional DPmix with CRP Backend
text: Covariate-informed DP mixtures predict outcome distributions shift x1 (covariates). Use predict(..., type = "density") visualize conditional densities type = "quantile" posterior-mean location shifts. Diagnostics (plot(fit_cond_normal), fitted(fit_cond_normal)) ensure chains mix relying predictions. Next vignette extends idea SB backend adding tails.

title: 11. Conditional DPmix with Stick-Breaking Backend
text: Purpose: Replace CRP backend stick-breaking truncation keeping covariate-dependent bulk structure. demonstrates fixed components interplay covariates.

title: 11. Conditional DPmix with Stick-Breaking Backend
text: Conditional Dataset Summary (SB)
code: data("nc_posX100_p3_k2") y <- nc_posX100_p3_k2$y X <- as.matrix(nc_posX100_p3_k2$X) if (is.null(colnames(X))) {   colnames(X) <- paste0("x", seq_len(ncol(X))) }  summary_tbl <- tibble(   statistic = c("N", "Mean", "SD", "Min", "Max"),   value = c(length(y), mean(y), sd(y), min(y), max(y)) )  ggplot(data.frame(y = y, x1 = X[, 1]), aes(x = x1, y = y)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.6, color = "darkorange") +   geom_smooth(method = "loess", color = "navy", fill = NA) +   labs(title = "y vs X1 (SB)", x = "X1", y = "y") +   theme_minimal()

title: 11. Conditional DPmix with Stick-Breaking Backend
code: bundle_sb_normal <- build_nimble_bundle(   y = y,   X = X,   kernel = "normal",   backend = "sb",   GPD = FALSE,   components = 5,   mcmc = mcmc )  bundle_sb_cauchy <- build_nimble_bundle(   y = y,   X = X,   kernel = "cauchy",   backend = "sb",   GPD = FALSE,   components = 5,   mcmc = mcmc )

title: 11. Conditional DPmix with Stick-Breaking Backend
code: X_new <- expand.grid(x1 = seq(-2, 2, length.out = 3), x2 = c(-1, 1), x3 = 0) colnames(X_new) <- colnames(X) y_min <- max(min(y), .Machine$double.eps) y_grid <- seq(y_min, max(y) * 1.1, length.out = 200)  densities_normal <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(X_new)), function(i) {   pred <- predict(fit_sb_normal, x = as.matrix(X_new[i, , drop = FALSE]), y = y_grid, type = "density")   data.frame(     y = pred$fit$y,     density = pred$fit$density,     label = paste0("x1=", round(X_new[i, "x1"], 1), ", x2=", X_new[i, "x2"]),     model = "Normal"   ) })  densities_cauchy <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(X_new)), function(i) {   pred <- predict(fit_sb_cauchy, x = as.matrix(X_new[i, , drop = FALSE]), y = y_grid, type = "density")   data.frame(     y = pred$fit$y,     density = pred$fit$density,     label = paste0("x1=", round(X_new[i, "x1"], 1), ", x2=", X_new[i, "x2"]),     model = "Cauchy"   ) })  df_cond <- bind_rows(densities_normal, densities_cauchy)  ggplot(df_cond, aes(x = y, y = density, color = label)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   facet_wrap(~ model) +   labs(title = "SB Conditional Predictive Densities", x = "y", y = "Density") +   theme_minimal() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

title: 11. Conditional DPmix with Stick-Breaking Backend

title: 11. Conditional DPmix with Stick-Breaking Backend
text: Stick-breaking component count fixed still supports covariate-dependent mixtures. predict(..., type = "density") returns group-specific densities X. predict(..., type = "quantile") reports posterior-mean quantiles; median 0.5 quantile shifts x1. Diagnoses rely S3 plot()/fitted() pipeline available vignettes.

code: X_new <- rbind(   c(-1, 0, 0, 0, 0),   c(0, 0, 0, 0, 0),   c(1, 1, 0, 0, 0) ) colnames(X_new) <- colnames(X) y_grid <- seq(0, max(y) * 1.2, length.out = 200)  df_pred_gamma <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(X_new)), function(i) {   pred <- predict(fit_sb_cond_gpd_gamma, x = as.matrix(X_new[i, , drop = FALSE]), y = y_grid, type = "density")   data.frame(     y = pred$fit$y,     density = pred$fit$density,     label = paste("x1=", X_new[i, 1], ", x2=", X_new[i, 2], sep = ""),     model = "Gamma"   ) })  df_pred_laplace <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(X_new)), function(i) {   pred <- predict(fit_sb_cond_gpd_laplace, x = as.matrix(X_new[i, , drop = FALSE]), y = y_grid, type = "density")   data.frame(     y = pred$fit$y,     density = pred$fit$density,     label = paste("x1=", X_new[i, 1], ", x2=", X_new[i, 2], sep = ""),     model = "Laplace"   ) })  bind_rows(df_pred_gamma, df_pred_laplace) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = y, y = density, color = label)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   facet_wrap(~ model) +   labs(title = "Conditional Density (SB + GPD)", x = "y", y = "Density") +   theme_minimal() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

title: 13. Conditional DPmixGPD with Stick-Breaking Backend
code: X_grid <- cbind(x1 = seq(-1, 1, length.out = 5), x2 = 0, x3 = 0, x4 = 0, x5 = 0) colnames(X_grid) <- colnames(X) quant_probs <- c(0.90, 0.95)  pred_q_gamma <- predict(fit_sb_cond_gpd_gamma, x = as.matrix(X_grid), type = "quantile", index = quant_probs) pred_q_laplace <- predict(fit_sb_cond_gpd_laplace, x = as.matrix(X_grid), type = "quantile", index = quant_probs)  quant_df_gamma <- pred_q_gamma$fit quant_df_gamma$x1 <- X_grid[quant_df_gamma$id, "x1"] quant_df_gamma$model <- "Gamma"  quant_df_laplace <- pred_q_laplace$fit quant_df_laplace$x1 <- X_grid[quant_df_laplace$id, "x1"] quant_df_laplace$model <- "Laplace"  bind_rows(quant_df_gamma, quant_df_laplace) %>%   ggplot(aes(x = x1, y = estimate, color = factor(index), group = index)) +   geom_line(linewidth = 1) +   geom_point(size = 2) +   facet_wrap(~ model) +   labs(title = "Tail Quantiles vs x1 (SB)", x = "x1", y = "Quantile", color = "Probability") +   theme_minimal()

title: 13. Conditional DPmixGPD with Stick-Breaking Backend
code: plot(fitted(fit_sb_cond_gpd_gamma)) plot(fit_sb_cond_gpd_gamma, family = "traceplot") === traceplot === plot(fit_sb_cond_gpd_laplace, family = "caterpillar") === caterpillar ===

title: 13. Conditional DPmixGPD with Stick-Breaking Backend
text: Conditional stick-breaking mixtures capture covariate-dependent bulk structure GPD handles extremes. predict() plot() remain consistent densities, posterior-mean quantiles, residuals. Expect threshold-selected posterior-mean tail quantiles shift x1 even components fixed. Next: move causal models starting -backend CRP (v12).

title: 14. Causal Inference: No X (CRP) - Gamma Kernel

what: Model B: CRP with GPD Tail (Gamma)
title: 14. Causal Inference: No X (CRP) - Gamma Kernel
code: param_specs_gpd <- list(   gpd = list(     threshold = list(       mode = "dist",       dist = "lognormal",       args = list(meanlog = log(max(u_threshold, .Machine$double.eps)), sdlog = 0.25)     )   ) )  bundle_crp_gpd <- tryCatch({   build_causal_bundle(     y = y,     T = T,     X = NULL,     kernel = "gamma",     backend = "crp",     PS = FALSE,     GPD = TRUE,     components = 6,     param_specs = param_specs_gpd,     mcmc_outcome = list(niter = 300, nburnin = 80, nchains = 1, thin = 1, seed = 2)   ) }, error = function(e) {   message("Skipping CRP + GPD example: ", conditionMessage(e))   NULL })  supports_crp_gpd <- !is.null(bundle_crp_gpd) if (supports_crp_gpd) bundle_crp_gpd if (isTRUE(supports_crp_gpd)) {   fit_crp_gpd <- quiet_mcmc(run_mcmc_causal(bundle_crp_gpd))   summary(fit_crp_gpd) } if (isTRUE(supports_crp_gpd)) {   pred_mean_gpd <- predict(fit_crp_gpd, type = "mean", interval = "credible", nsim_mean = 150)   head(pred_mean_gpd)   plot(pred_mean_gpd) } if (isTRUE(supports_crp_gpd)) {   pred_q_gpd <- predict(fit_crp_gpd, type = "quantile", p = 0.5, interval = "credible")   head(pred_q_gpd)   plot(pred_q_gpd) } if (isTRUE(supports_crp_gpd)) {   pred_d_gpd <- predict(fit_crp_gpd, y = y_eval, type = "density", interval = "credible")   head(pred_d_gpd)   plot(pred_d_gpd) } if (isTRUE(supports_crp_gpd)) {   pred_surv_gpd <- predict(fit_crp_gpd, y = y_eval, type = "survival", interval = "credible")   head(pred_surv_gpd)   plot(pred_surv_gpd) } if (isTRUE(supports_crp_gpd)) {   ate_gpd <- ate(fit_crp_gpd, interval = "credible", nsim_mean = 150)   print(ate_gpd)   summary(ate_gpd) } if (isTRUE(supports_crp_gpd)) {   ate_plots_gpd <- plot(ate_gpd)   ate_plots_gpd$treatment_effect } if (isTRUE(supports_crp_gpd)) {   qte_gpd <- qte(fit_crp_gpd, probs = c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75), interval = "credible")   print(qte_gpd)   summary(qte_gpd) } if (isTRUE(supports_crp_gpd)) {   qte_plots_gpd <- plot(qte_gpd)   qte_plots_gpd$treatment_effect }

title: 16. Causal Inference: Mixed Backends (CRP+SB) - Cauchy/Laplace
text: vignette fits two causal models using different backends kernels: Model : CRP bulk-(Cauchy) Model B: SB GPD tail (Laplace) model demonstrates full causal S3 workflow: bundle, fit, predict, plot, ATE, QTE.

title: 16. Causal Inference: Mixed Backends (CRP+SB) - Cauchy/Laplace
text: Causal Dataset Summary
code: data("causal_alt_real500_p4_k2") y <- causal_alt_real500_p4_k2$y T <- causal_alt_real500_p4_k2$T X <- as.matrix(causal_alt_real500_p4_k2$X)  summary_tbl <- tibble(   statistic = c("N", "Mean", "SD", "Min", "Max"),   value = c(length(y), mean(y), sd(y), min(y), max(y)) )  summary_tbl %>%   mutate(value = signif(value, 4)) %>%   kable(caption = "Causal Dataset Summary", align = "c") %>%   kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE, position = "center") x_eval <- X[1:40, , drop = FALSE] y_eval <- y[1:40] u_threshold <- as.numeric(stats::quantile(y, 0.8, names = FALSE)) df_causal <- data.frame(y = y, T = as.factor(T), x1 = X[, 1], x2 = X[, 2])  p_scatter <- ggplot(df_causal, aes(x = x1, y = y, color = T)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +   labs(title = "Outcome vs X1 by Treatment", x = "X1", y = "y", color = "Treatment") +   theme_minimal()  p_scatter

title: 16. Causal Inference: Mixed Backends (CRP+SB) - Cauchy/Laplace
code: bundle_crp_bulk <- build_causal_bundle(   y = y,   T = T,   X = X,   kernel = "cauchy",   backend = "crp",   PS = "logit",   GPD = FALSE,   components = 5,   mcmc_outcome = list(niter = 2500, nburnin = 500, nchains = 1, thin = 1, seed = 1),   mcmc_ps = list(niter = 2500, nburnin = 500, nchains = 1, thin = 1, seed = 1) )  bundle_crp_bulk DPmixGPD causal bundle PS model: disabled  Outcome (treated): backend = crp | kernel = cauchy  Outcome (control): backend = crp | kernel = cauchy  GPD tail (treated/control): FALSE / FALSE  components (treated/control): 5 / 5  Outcome PS included: FALSE  epsilon (treated/control): 0.025 / 0.025  n (control) = 232 | n (treated) = 268 summary(bundle_crp_bulk) DPmixGPD causal bundle summary DPmixGPD causal bundle PS model: disabled  Outcome (treated): backend = crp | kernel = cauchy  Outcome (control): backend = crp | kernel = cauchy  GPD tail (treated/control): FALSE / FALSE  components (treated/control): 5 / 5  Outcome PS included: FALSE  epsilon (treated/control): 0.025 / 0.025  n (control) = 232 | n (treated) = 268 fit_crp_bulk <- quiet_mcmc(run_mcmc_causal(bundle_crp_bulk)) summary(fit_crp_bulk) -- Outcome fits -- [control] MixGPD fit | backend: Chinese Restaurant Process | kernel: Cauchy Distribution | GPD tail: FALSE n = 232 | components = 5 | epsilon = 0.025 MCMC: niter=2500, nburnin=500, thin=1, nchains=1  Fit Use summary() for posterior summaries; plot() for diagnostics; predict() for predictions. code: data("causal_alt_real500_p4_k2") y <- causal_alt_real500_p4_k2$y T <- causal_alt_real500_p4_k2$T X <- as.matrix(causal_alt_real500_p4_k2$X)  summary_tbl <- tibble(   statistic = c("N", "Mean", "SD", "Min", "Max"),   value = c(length(y), mean(y), sd(y), min(y), max(y)) )  summary_tbl %>%   mutate(value = signif(value, 4)) %>%   kable(caption = "Causal Dataset Summary", align = "c") %>%   kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE, position = "center") x_eval <- X[1:40, , drop = FALSE] y_eval <- y[1:40] u_threshold <- as.numeric(stats::quantile(y, 0.8, names = FALSE)) df_causal <- data.frame(y = y, T = as.factor(T), x1 = X[, 1], x2 = X[, 2])  p_scatter <- ggplot(df_causal, aes(x = x1, y = y, color = T)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +   labs(title = "Outcome vs X1 by Treatment", x = "X1", y = "y", color = "Treatment") +   theme_minimal()  p_scatter

title: 19. Causal Inference: Mixed Backends with GPD - Amoroso Kernel
text: Shifted Outcome Summary (Amoroso)
code: data("causal_alt_real500_p4_k2") y_raw <- causal_alt_real500_p4_k2$y T <- causal_alt_real500_p4_k2$T X <- as.matrix(causal_alt_real500_p4_k2$X)  shift <- abs(min(y_raw)) + 0.1 y <- y_raw + shift  summary_tbl <- tibble(   statistic = c("N", "Mean", "SD", "Min", "Max"),   value = c(length(y), mean(y), sd(y), min(y), max(y)) )  summary_tbl %>%   mutate(value = signif(value, 4)) %>%   kable(caption = "Shifted Outcome Summary (Amoroso)", align = "c") %>%   kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE, position = "center") x_eval <- X[1:40, , drop = FALSE] y_eval <- y[1:40] u_threshold <- as.numeric(stats::quantile(y, 0.8, names = FALSE)) df_causal <- data.frame(y = y, T = as.factor(T), x1 = X[, 1])  p_scatter <- ggplot(df_causal, aes(x = x1, y = y, color = T)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +   labs(title = "Shifted Outcome vs X1", x = "X1", y = "y (shifted)", color = "Treatment") +   theme_minimal()  p_scatter

title: 20. Troubleshooting
text: vignette collects common errors fixes encountered building running DPmixGPD models.

what: 1. “Number of columns in ‘x’ does not match training design matrix”
title: 20. Troubleshooting
text: Cause: New data different columns training X. Fix: Use column names order training matrix.
code: X_train <- fit$data$X X_new <- X_train[1:5, , drop = FALSE] X_new <- X_new[, colnames(X_train), drop = FALSE]  predict(fit, x = X_new, type = "mean")

what: 2. “Cauchy kernels are never paired with GPD tails”
title: 20. Troubleshooting
text: Cause: Cauchy kernel allowed GPD tails. Fix: Use different kernel disable GPD.
code: bundle <- build_nimble_bundle(   y = y,   kernel = "cauchy",   backend = "sb",   GPD = FALSE )

title: 20. Troubleshooting
text: Cause: Observational causal modeling requires covariates PS estimation. Fix: Provide X, switch design = "rct".
code: cb <- build_causal_bundle(   y = y,   T = T,   X = X,   design = "observational",   PS = "logit" )

title: 20. Troubleshooting
text: Cause: short chains overly restrictive priors. Fix: Increase iterations relax tail priors.
code: bundle <- build_nimble_bundle(   y = y,   kernel = "gamma",   backend = "crp",   GPD = TRUE,   components = 6,   mcmc = mcmc )

title: 20. Troubleshooting
text: Cause: Parallel predictions export large functions/objects. Fix: Set higher limit use ncores = 1.
code: options(future.globals.maxSize = 4 * 1024^3)  pred <- predict(fit, x = X_new, y = y_grid, type = "density", ncores = 2)

title: 20. Troubleshooting
text: Cause: Large datasets, many components, heavy kernels. Fix: Use smaller components/J, shorter niter, start CRP.
code: bundle <- build_nimble_bundle(   y = y,   backend = "crp",   kernel = "normal",   components = 4,   mcmc = mcmc )

what: 7. Prediction returns constant fitted values in unconditional models
title: 20. Troubleshooting
text: Cause: Unconditional fitted values model-based population summaries. Fix: expected. Use conditional models need observation-specific fitted values.
code: fit_vals <- fitted(fit) head(fit_vals)

what: 8. PS not used in causal model
title: 20. Troubleshooting
text: Cause: PS=FALSE X missing. Fix: Provide X enable PS.
code: cb <- build_causal_bundle(   y = y,   T = T,   X = X,   PS = "logit" ) fit <- run_mcmc_causal(cb)

title: 20. Troubleshooting
text: Cause: Using kernels different supports across arms. Fix: Ensure arms use kernels support (e.g., positive-support real-line).
code: cb <- build_causal_bundle(   y = y,   T = T,   X = X,   kernel = c("gamma", "lognormal"),   backend = c("sb", "crp") )

title: Reference: S3 Methods
text: ate() qte() functions return objects S3 methods.

title: Reference: S3 Methods
code: n <- 60 X <- data.frame(x = rnorm(n)) T_ind <- rbinom(n, 1, plogis(0.2 + 0.5 * X$x)) y0 <- 0.5 + 0.7 * X$x + abs(rnorm(n)) + 0.1 te <- 0.4 + 0.6 * (X$x > 0) y1 <- y0 + te y <- ifelse(T_ind == 1, y1, y0)  causal_bundle <- build_causal_bundle(   y = y,   X = X,   T = T_ind,   backend = "sb",   kernel = "normal",   GPD = TRUE,   components = 6,   PS = "logit",   design = "observational",   mcmc_outcome = mcmc,   mcmc_ps = mcmc ) causal_fit <- run_mcmc_causal(causal_bundle, show_progress = FALSE) #> ===== Monitors ===== #> thin = 1: beta #> ===== Samplers ===== #> RW sampler (2) #>   - beta[]  (2 elements) #> [MCMC] Creating NIMBLE model... #> [MCMC] NIMBLE model created successfully. #> [MCMC] Configuring MCMC... #> ===== Monitors ===== #> thin = 1: alpha, beta_mean, beta_ps_mean, beta_tail_scale, beta_threshold, sd, sdlog_u, tail_shape, threshold, w, z #> ===== Samplers ===== #> RW sampler (53) #>   - alpha #>   - sd[]  (6 elements) #>   - beta_mean[]  (6 elements) #>   - beta_ps_mean[]  (6 elements) #>   - sdlog_u #>   - beta_tail_scale[]  (1 element) #>   - tail_shape #>   - v[]  (5 elements) #>   - threshold[]  (26 elements) #> conjugate sampler (1) #>   - beta_threshold[]  (1 element) #> categorical sampler (26) #>   - z[]  (26 elements) #> [MCMC] MCMC configured. #> [MCMC] Building MCMC object... #> [MCMC] MCMC object built. #> [MCMC] Attempting NIMBLE compilation (this may take a minute)... #> [MCMC] Compiling model... #> [MCMC] Compiling MCMC sampler... #> [MCMC] Compilation successful. #> [MCMC] MCMC execution complete. Processing results... #> [MCMC] Creating NIMBLE model... #> [MCMC] NIMBLE model created successfully. #> [MCMC] Configuring MCMC... #> ===== Monitors ===== #> thin = 1: alpha, beta_mean, beta_ps_mean, beta_tail_scale, beta_threshold, sd, sdlog_u, tail_shape, threshold, w, z #> ===== Samplers ===== #> RW sampler (61) #>   - alpha #>   - sd[]  (6 elements) #>   - beta_mean[]  (6 elements) #>   - beta_ps_mean[]  (6 elements) #>   - sdlog_u #>   - beta_tail_scale[]  (1 element) #>   - tail_shape #>   - v[]  (5 elements) #>   - threshold[]  (34 elements) #> conjugate sampler (1) #>   - beta_threshold[]  (1 element) #> categorical sampler (34) #>   - z[]  (34 elements) #> [MCMC] MCMC configured. #> [MCMC] Building MCMC object... #> [MCMC] MCMC object built. #> [MCMC] Attempting NIMBLE compilation (this may take a minute)... #> [MCMC] Compiling model... #> [MCMC] Compiling MCMC sampler... #> [MCMC] Compilation successful. #> [MCMC] MCMC execution complete. (54 more rows) summary(ate_result) #> ATE Summary #> ==================================================  #> Prediction points: 60 #> Conditional: YES | PS used: YES #> Posterior mean draws: 50 #> Interval: hpd #>  #> Model specification: #>   Backend (trt/con): sb / sb #>   Kernel (trt/con): normal / normal #>   GPD tail (trt/con): YES / YES #>  #> ATE statistics: #>   Mean: -1.257 | Median: -0.784 #>   Range: [-5.691, 0.029] #>   SD: 1.09 #>  #> Credible interval width: #>   Mean: 4.105 | Median: 2.821 #>   Range: [1.82, 17.242] ate_plots <- plot(ate_result) ate_plots$treatment_effect plot(ate_result, type = "effect")

title: Unconditional Models

title: Unconditional Models
code: library(DPmixGPD)  n <- 100 y <- abs(rnorm(n)) + 0.2  bundle <- build_nimble_bundle(   y = y,   backend = "sb",   kernel = "normal",   GPD = TRUE,   components = 6,   mcmc = mcmc )  fit <- run_mcmc_bundle_manual(bundle, show_progress = FALSE) #> [MCMC] Creating NIMBLE model... #> [MCMC] NIMBLE model created successfully. #> [MCMC] Configuring MCMC... #> ===== Monitors ===== #> thin = 1: alpha, mean, sd, tail_scale, tail_shape, threshold, w, z #> ===== Samplers ===== #> RW sampler (21) #>   - alpha #>   - mean[]  (6 elements) #>   - sd[]  (6 elements) #>   - threshold #>   - tail_scale #>   - tail_shape #>   - v[]  (5 elements) #> categorical sampler (100) #>   - z[]  (100 elements) #> [MCMC] MCMC configured. #> [MCMC] Building MCMC object... #> [MCMC] MCMC object built. #> [MCMC] Attempting NIMBLE compilation (this may take a minute)... #> [MCMC] Compiling model... #> [MCMC] Compiling MCMC sampler... #> [MCMC] Compilation successful. #> [MCMC] MCMC execution complete. Processing results... fit #> MixGPD fit | backend: Stick-Breaking Process | kernel: Normal Distribution | GPD tail: TRUE #> n = 100 | components = 6 | epsilon = 0.025 #> MCMC: niter=400, nburnin=100, thin=2, nchains=1  #> Fit #> Use summary() for posterior summaries; plot() for diagnostics; predict() for predictions.

code: pred_mean <- predict(fit, type = "mean", cred.level = 0.90, interval = "credible") pred_q95  <- predict(fit, type = "quantile", index = 0.95, cred.level = 0.90, interval = "credible")  pred_mean$fit #>   estimate     lower  upper #> 1 1.365748 0.9977448 2.0355 pred_q95$fit #>   estimate index    lower    upper #> 1 2.056073  0.95 1.788326 2.348427

what: Dirichlet Process Mixtures with Generalized Pareto Tail
title: Dirichlet Process Mixtures with Generalized Pareto Tail
text: DPmixGPD fits Dirichlet process mixture models (CRP stick-breaking backends) optional Generalized Pareto (GPD) tail, enabling unified modeling bulk distributions extreme values clean, user-friendly prediction APIs.

title: Amoroso distribution — amoroso
text: Base Amoroso distribution functions implemented package. Function names parameterization follow existing Amoroso implementation. d*, p*, q* functions accept vector inputs first argument evaluate elementwise; r* supports n > 1.

title: Amoroso distribution — amoroso
code: dAmoroso(x, loc, scale, shape1, shape2, log = 0)  pAmoroso(q, loc, scale, shape1, shape2, lower.tail = 1, log.p = 0)  qAmoroso(p, loc, scale, shape1, shape2, lower.tail = TRUE, log.p = FALSE)  rAmoroso(n, loc, scale, shape1, shape2)

title: Amoroso distribution — amoroso
text: x Numeric scalar giving point density evaluated. loc Numeric scalar location parameter. scale Numeric scalar scale parameter. shape1 Numeric scalar first shape parameter. shape2 Numeric scalar second shape parameter. log Logical; TRUE, return log-density (integer flag 0/1 NIMBLE). q Numeric scalar giving point distribution function evaluated. lower.tail Logical; TRUE (default), probabilities \(P(X \le q)\). log.p Logical; TRUE, probabilities returned log scale. p Numeric scalar probability \((0,1)\) quantile function. n Integer giving number draws. portability inside NIMBLE, RNG implementation supports n = 1.

title: Amoroso distribution — amoroso
text: Density/CDF/RNG functions return numeric scalars. quantile function returns numeric scalar.

title: Amoroso distribution — amoroso
text: dAmoroso(): Density Function Amoroso Distribution pAmoroso(): Distribution Function Amoroso Distribution qAmoroso(): Quantile Function Amoroso Distribution rAmoroso(): Sample generating Function Amoroso Distribution

title: Amoroso mixture distribution — amoroso_mix
text: finite mixture Amoroso components often convenient bulk model response positive right-skewed. functions evaluate simulate \(\sum_j w_j f_{Amoroso}(\cdot\mid loc_j, scale_j, shape1_j, shape2_j)\).

what: Amoroso mixture with a GPD tail — amoroso_mixgpd
title: Amoroso mixture with a GPD tail — amoroso_mixgpd
text: family splices generalized Pareto distribution (GPD) threshold threshold onto Amoroso mixture bulk. Let \(F_{mix}\) denote Amoroso mixture CDF. spliced CDF \(F(x)=F_{mix}(x)\) \(x<threshold\) \(F(x)=F_{mix}(threshold) + \left\{1-F_{mix}(threshold)\right\}G(x)\) \(x\ge threshold\), \(G\) GPD CDF exceedances threshold.

title: Amoroso mixture with a GPD tail — amoroso_mixgpd

title: Amoroso mixture with a GPD tail — amoroso_mixgpd
code: w <- c(0.60, 0.25, 0.15) loc <- c(0, 1, 2) scale <- c(1.0, 1.2, 1.6) shape1 <- c(2, 4, 6) shape2 <- c(1.0, 1.2, 1.5) threshold <- 3 tail_scale <- 1.0 tail_shape <- 0.2  dAmorosoMixGpd(4.0, w, loc, scale, shape1, shape2,               threshold, tail_scale, tail_shape, log = 0) #> [1] 0.1642462 pAmorosoMixGpd(4.0, w, loc, scale, shape1, shape2,               threshold, tail_scale, tail_shape, lower.tail = 1, log.p = 0) #> [1] 0.8029046 qAmorosoMixGpd(0.50, w, loc, scale, shape1, shape2,               threshold, tail_scale, tail_shape) #> [1] 2.929829 qAmorosoMixGpd(0.95, w, loc, scale, shape1, shape2,               threshold, tail_scale, tail_shape) #> [1] 5.893917 replicate(10, rAmorosoMixGpd(1, w, loc, scale, shape1, shape2,                             threshold, tail_scale, tail_shape)) #>  [1] 3.3304140 3.4262481 3.2171814 3.8463938 3.3374576 0.2579286 0.2150753 #>  [8] 3.4350296 2.0246966 1.6155426

title: Average treatment effects (ATE) — ate
text: Computes treated-minus-control posterior means causal fit.

title: Average treatment effects (ATE) — ate
code: ate(fit, newdata = NULL, interval = "credible", level = 0.95, nsim_mean = 200L)

title: Average treatment effects (ATE) — ate
text: fit "dpmixgpd_causal_fit" object run_mcmc_causal(). newdata Optional data.frame matrix covariates prediction. interval Character NULL; type credible interval: NULL interval, "credible" equal-tailed quantile intervals (default), "hpd" highest posterior density intervals. level Numeric credible level intervals (default 0.95 95 percent CI). nsim_mean Number posterior predictive draws approximate mean.

title: Average treatment effects (ATE) — ate
text: list elements fit (ATE), optional lower/upper, treated/control prediction objects.

title: Average treatment effects (ATE) — ate
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cb <- build_causal_bundle(y = y, X = X, T = T, backend = "sb", kernel = "normal", J = 6) fit <- run_mcmc_causal(cb, show_progress = FALSE) ate(fit, newdata = X[1:5, ]) ate(fit, interval = "credible", level = 0.90)  # 90% CI ate(fit, interval = "hpd")  # HPD intervals ate(fit, interval = NULL)   # No intervals } # }

what: Lowercase vectorized distribution functions (base kernels) — base_lowercase
title: Lowercase vectorized distribution functions (base kernels) — base_lowercase
text: Vectorized R wrappers base distribution functions. lowercase versions accept vector inputs first argument (x, q, p) return numeric vector. r* functions support n > 1 return numeric vector length n.

title: Lowercase vectorized distribution functions (base kernels) — base_lowercase
code: dgpd(x, threshold, scale, shape, log = FALSE)  pgpd(q, threshold, scale, shape, lower.tail = TRUE, log.p = FALSE)  qgpd(p, threshold, scale, shape, lower.tail = TRUE, log.p = FALSE)  rgpd(n, threshold, scale, shape)  dinvgauss(x, mean, shape, log = FALSE)  pinvgauss(q, mean, shape, lower.tail = TRUE, log.p = FALSE)  qinvgauss(   p,   mean,   shape,   lower.tail = TRUE,   log.p = FALSE,   tol = 1e-10,   maxiter = 200 )  rinvgauss(n, mean, shape)  damoroso(x, loc, scale, shape1, shape2, log = FALSE)  pamoroso(q, loc, scale, shape1, shape2, lower.tail = TRUE, log.p = FALSE)  qamoroso(p, loc, scale, shape1, shape2, lower.tail = TRUE, log.p = FALSE)  ramoroso(n, loc, scale, shape1, shape2)  dcauchy_vec(x, location, scale, log = FALSE)  pcauchy_vec(q, location, scale, lower.tail = TRUE, log.p = FALSE)  qcauchy_vec(p, location, scale, lower.tail = TRUE, log.p = FALSE)  rcauchy_vec(n, location, scale)

title: Lowercase vectorized distribution functions (base kernels) — base_lowercase
