what: Build a causal bundle (design + two outcome arms) — build_causal_bundle
title: Build a causal bundle (design + two outcome arms) — build_causal_bundle
text: Creates causal bundle : propensity score (PS) design model (logit/probit regression T X naive Bayes classifier) outcome bundle control arm (T = 0) outcome bundle treated arm (T = 1)

title: Build a causal bundle (design + two outcome arms) — build_causal_bundle
code: build_causal_bundle(   y,   X,   T,   backend = c("sb", "crp"),   kernel,   GPD = FALSE,   components = NULL,   J = NULL,   param_specs = NULL,   mcmc_outcome = list(niter = 2000, nburnin = 500, thin = 1, nchains = 1, seed = 1),   mcmc_ps = list(niter = 2000, nburnin = 500, thin = 1, nchains = 1, seed = 1),   epsilon = 0.025,   alpha_random = TRUE,   ps_model = c("logit"),   ps_prior = list(mean = 0, sd = 2),   include_intercept = TRUE,   PS = "logit",   design = c("rct", "observational"),   ps_scale = c("logit", "prob"),   ps_summary = c("mean", "median"),   ps_clamp = 1e-06 )

title: Build a causal bundle (design + two outcome arms) — build_causal_bundle
text: y Numeric outcome vector. X Design matrix data.frame covariates (N x P). T Binary treatment indicator (length N, values 0/1). backend Character; "sb" "crp" outcome models. length 2, first entry used treated (T=1) second control (T=0). kernel Character kernel name outcome models. length 2, first entry used treated (T=1) second control (T=0). GPD Logical; include GPD tail outcomes TRUE. length 2, first entry used treated (T=1) second control (T=0). components Deprecated alias J. one J components supplied. J Integer >= 2; truncation parameter outcome mixtures. length 2, first entry used treated (T=1) second control (T=0). param_specs Outcome parameter overrides (structure build_nimble_bundle()). can pass single list used arms list con trt entries. mcmc_outcome MCMC settings list outcome bundles. mcmc_ps MCMC settings list PS model. epsilon Numeric [0,1) used outcome bundles posterior truncation summaries. length 2, first entry used treated (T=1) second control (T=0). alpha_random Logical; whether outcome concentration alpha stochastic. ps_model PS model specification backward compatibility (deprecated). Recommend using PS parameter instead. ps_prior Normal prior PS coefficients. List mean sd. include_intercept Logical; TRUE, intercept column prepended X PS model. PS Character logical; controls propensity score estimation: "logit" (default): Logistic regression PS model "probit": Probit regression PS model "naive": Gaussian naive Bayes PS model FALSE: PS estimation; outcome models use X PS model choice stored bundle metadata downstream use prediction summaries, enabling seamless integration future PS estimation methods. design Causal design: "rct" "observational". ps_scale Scale used augmenting outcomes PS: "logit" "prob". ps_summary Posterior summary PS: "mean" "median". ps_clamp Numeric epsilon clamping PS values \((\epsilon, 1-\epsilon)\).

title: Build a causal bundle (design + two outcome arms) — build_causal_bundle
text: list class "dpmixgpd_causal_bundle".

title: Build a causal bundle (design + two outcome arms) — build_causal_bundle
text: outcome bundles reuse existing DPM + optional GPD tail machinery. PS model lightweight NIMBLE estimator supporting logit/probit regression naive Bayes classifier simple priors.

title: Build a causal bundle (design + two outcome arms) — build_causal_bundle
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ set.seed(1) N <- 100 X <- cbind(x1 = rnorm(N), x2 = runif(N)) T <- rbinom(N, 1, plogis(0.3 + 0.5 * X[, 1])) y <- rexp(N) + 0.1  cb <- build_causal_bundle(   y = y,   X = X,   T = T,   backend = "sb",   kernel = "gamma",   GPD = TRUE,   J = 10,   PS = "probit" ) } # }

title: Build a NIMBLE bundle — build_nimble_bundle
text: Creates runnable "bundle" containing: compiled model spec nimbleCode model code constants, data, explicit dimensions initialization function inits (stored function) monitor specification MCMC settings list (stored used code generation)

title: Build a NIMBLE bundle — build_nimble_bundle
code: build_nimble_bundle(   y,   X = NULL,   ps = NULL,   backend = c("sb", "crp"),   kernel,   GPD = FALSE,   components = NULL,   J = NULL,   param_specs = NULL,   mcmc = list(niter = 2000, nburnin = 500, thin = 1, nchains = 1, seed = 1),   epsilon = 0.025,   alpha_random = TRUE )

title: Build a NIMBLE bundle — build_nimble_bundle
text: y Numeric outcome vector. X Optional design matrix/data.frame (N x p) conditional variants. ps Optional numeric vector (length N) propensity scores. provided, augments design matrix PS-adjusted outcome modeling. backend Character; "sb" (stick-breaking) "crp" (Chinese Restaurant Process). kernel Character kernel name (must exist get_kernel_registry()). GPD Logical; whether GPD tail requested. components Deprecated alias J. one J components supplied. J Integer >= 2. Single user-facing truncation parameter: SB: number mixture components used stick-breaking truncation CRP: maximum number clusters represented finite NIMBLE model param_specs Optional list entries bulk tail override defaults. mcmc Named list MCMC settings (niter, nburnin, thin, nchains, seed). Stored bundle. epsilon Numeric [0,1). downstream summaries/plots/prediction keep smaller k defined either () cumulative mass >= 1 - epsilon (ii) per-component weights >= epsilon, renormalize. alpha_random Logical; whether concentration alpha stochastic.

title: Build a NIMBLE bundle — build_nimble_bundle
text: named list (bundle) class "dpmixgpd_bundle".

title: Build a NIMBLE bundle — build_nimble_bundle
text: function intentionally stops "pre-run" stage (spec/code/constants/data/dimensions/inits/monitors). Use run_mcmc_bundle_manual() execute MCMC stored settings.

title: Build a NIMBLE bundle — build_nimble_bundle
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ y <- abs(rnorm(60)) + 0.1 bundle <- build_nimble_bundle(   y = y,   backend = "sb",   kernel = "normal",   GPD = FALSE,   components = 4,   mcmc = list(niter = 200, nburnin = 50, thin = 1, nchains = 1, seed = 1) ) bundle } # }

title: Cauchy distribution — cauchy
text: Base Cauchy distribution functions implemented nimbleFunctions can used NIMBLE models. Parameterization uses location scale (scale > 0). d*, p*, q* functions accept vector inputs first argument evaluate elementwise; r* supports n > 1.

title: Cauchy distribution — cauchy
code: dCauchy(x, location, scale, log = 0)  pCauchy(q, location, scale, lower.tail = 1, log.p = 0)  rCauchy(n, location, scale)  qCauchy(p, location, scale, lower.tail = TRUE, log.p = FALSE)

title: Cauchy distribution — cauchy
text: x Numeric scalar giving point density evaluated. location Numeric scalar location parameter. scale Numeric scalar scale parameter; must positive. log Logical; TRUE, return log-density (integer flag 0/1 NIMBLE). q Numeric scalar giving point distribution function evaluated. lower.tail Logical; TRUE (default), probabilities \(P(X \le q)\). log.p Logical; TRUE, probabilities returned log scale. n Integer giving number draws. portability inside NIMBLE, RNG implementation supports n = 1. p Numeric scalar probability \((0,1)\) quantile function.

title: Cauchy distribution — cauchy
text: dCauchy returns numeric scalar density; pCauchy returns numeric scalar CDF; rCauchy returns one random draw; qCauchy returns numeric quantile.

title: Cauchy distribution — cauchy
text: dCauchy(): Cauchy density function pCauchy(): Cauchy distribution function rCauchy(): Cauchy random generation qCauchy(): Cauchy quantile function

title: Cauchy mixture distribution — cauchy_mix
text: finite mixture Cauchy components. Base Cauchy functions taken stats. mixture density distribution function computed weighted sums. Random generation samples component index according weights draws corresponding component. Quantiles computed numerical inversion mixture CDF. d*, p*, q* functions accept vector inputs first argument evaluate elementwise; r* supports n > 1.

title: Cauchy mixture distribution — cauchy_mix
code: dCauchyMix(x, w, location, scale, log = 0)  pCauchyMix(q, w, location, scale, lower.tail = 1, log.p = 0)  rCauchyMix(n, w, location, scale)  qCauchyMix(   p,   w,   location,   scale,   lower.tail = TRUE,   log.p = FALSE,   tol = 1e-10,   maxiter = 200 )

title: Cauchy mixture distribution — cauchy_mix
text: x Numeric scalar giving point density evaluated. w Numeric vector mixture weights length \(K\). functions normalize w internally needed. location, scale Numeric vectors length \(K\) giving component locations scales. log Logical; TRUE, return log-density. q Numeric scalar giving point distribution function evaluated. lower.tail Logical; TRUE (default), probabilities \(P(X \le q)\). log.p Logical; TRUE, probabilities returned log scale. n Integer giving number draws. RNG implementation supports n = 1. p Numeric scalar probability \((0,1)\) quantile function. tol Numeric scalar tolerance passed stats::uniroot. maxiter Integer maximum number iterations stats::uniroot.

title: Cauchy mixture distribution — cauchy_mix
text: Density/CDF/RNG functions return numeric scalars. qCauchyMix returns numeric vector length p.

title: Cauchy mixture distribution — cauchy_mix
text: dCauchyMix(): Cauchy mixture density pCauchyMix(): Cauchy mixture distribution function rCauchyMix(): Cauchy mixture random generation qCauchyMix(): Cauchy mixture quantile function

title: Cauchy mixture distribution — cauchy_mix
code: w <- c(0.50, 0.30, 0.20) location <- c(-2, 0, 3) scale <- c(1.0, 0.7, 1.5)  dCauchyMix(0.5, w = w, location = location, scale = scale, log = FALSE) #> [1] 0.1235181 pCauchyMix(0.5, w = w, location = location, scale = scale,            lower.tail = TRUE, log.p = FALSE) #> [1] 0.6830742 qCauchyMix(0.50, w = w, location = location, scale = scale) #> [1] -0.6639507 qCauchyMix(0.95, w = w, location = location, scale = scale) #> [1] 6.996407 replicate(10, rCauchyMix(1, w = w, location = location, scale = scale)) #>  [1]   0.02594232   4.15434700 -38.57465351 133.69621213  -0.33217389 #>  [6]   0.19025854   1.47153823  -0.68694099   6.38862006  -2.31494157

Unused.

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ y <- abs(stats::rnorm(50)) + 0.1 bundle <- build_nimble_bundle(y = y, backend = "sb", kernel = "normal",                              GPD = TRUE, components = 6,                              mcmc = list(niter = 200, nburnin = 50, thin = 1, nchains = 1)) fit <- run_mcmc_bundle_manual(bundle) fitted(fit) fitted(fit, level = 0.90) fitted(fit, interval = "hpd")  # HPD intervals fitted(fit, interval = NULL)   # No intervals } # }

what: Gamma with a GPD tail — gamma_gpd
title: Gamma with a GPD tail — gamma_gpd
text: Splices generalized Pareto distribution (GPD) threshold onto single Gamma bulk distribution parameterized scale scale shape shape. bulk probability threshold, \(F_{bulk}(threshold)\), scales tail mass overall CDF proper.

title: Gamma with a GPD tail — gamma_gpd
code: dGammaGpd(x, shape, scale, threshold, tail_scale, tail_shape, log = 0)  pGammaGpd(   q,   shape,   scale,   threshold,   tail_scale,   tail_shape,   lower.tail = 1,   log.p = 0 )  rGammaGpd(n, shape, scale, threshold, tail_scale, tail_shape)  qGammaGpd(   p,   shape,   scale,   threshold,   tail_scale,   tail_shape,   lower.tail = TRUE,   log.p = FALSE,   tol = 1e-10,   maxiter = 200 )

title: Gamma with a GPD tail — gamma_gpd
text: x Numeric scalar giving point density evaluated. shape Numeric scalar Gamma shape parameter. scale Numeric scalar scale parameter Gamma bulk. threshold Numeric scalar threshold GPD tail attached. tail_scale Numeric scalar GPD scale parameter; must positive. tail_shape Numeric scalar GPD shape parameter. log Integer flag 0/1; 1, return log-density. q Numeric scalar giving point distribution function evaluated. lower.tail Integer flag 0/1; 1 (default), probabilities \(P(X \le q)\). log.p Integer flag 0/1; 1, probabilities returned log scale. n Integer giving number draws. RNG implementation supports n = 1. p Numeric scalar probability \((0,1)\) quantile function. tol Numeric tolerance numerical inversion qGammaGpd. maxiter Maximum iterations numerical inversion qGammaGpd.

title: Gamma with a GPD tail — gamma_gpd
text: Spliced density/CDF/RNG functions return numeric scalars. qGammaGpd returns numeric vector length p.

title: Gamma with a GPD tail — gamma_gpd
text: dGammaGpd(): Gamma + GPD tail density pGammaGpd(): Gamma + GPD tail distribution function rGammaGpd(): Gamma + GPD tail random generation qGammaGpd(): Gamma + GPD tail quantile function

title: Gamma with a GPD tail — gamma_gpd
code: scale <- 2.5 shape <- 4 threshold <- 3 tail_scale <- 0.9 tail_shape <- 0.2  dGammaGpd(4.0, scale = scale, shape = shape,          threshold = threshold, tail_scale = tail_scale,          tail_shape = tail_shape, log = 0) #> [1] 0.3220605 pGammaGpd(4.0, scale = scale, shape = shape,          threshold = threshold, tail_scale = tail_scale,          tail_shape = tail_shape, lower.tail = 1, log.p = 0) #> [1] 0.6457335 qGammaGpd(0.50, scale = scale, shape = shape,          threshold = threshold, tail_scale = tail_scale,          tail_shape = tail_shape) #> [1] 3.63375 qGammaGpd(0.95, scale = scale, shape = shape,          threshold = threshold, tail_scale = tail_scale,          tail_shape = tail_shape) #> [1] 6.636445 replicate(10, rGammaGpd(1, scale = scale, shape = shape,                        threshold = threshold,                        tail_scale = tail_scale,                        tail_shape = tail_shape)) #>  [1] 3.041267 3.066459 3.937674 5.774885 3.887805 5.725403 4.224517 4.338427 #>  [9] 3.583110 3.823371

what: Lowercase vectorized Gamma distribution functions — gamma_lowercase
title: Lowercase vectorized Gamma distribution functions — gamma_lowercase
text: Vectorized R wrappers Gamma mixture, Gamma mixture + GPD, Gamma + GPD distribution functions. lowercase versions accept vector inputs first argument (x, q, p) return numeric vector. r* functions support n > 1.

title: Lowercase vectorized Gamma distribution functions — gamma_lowercase
code: dgammamix(x, w, shape, scale, log = FALSE)  pgammamix(q, w, shape, scale, lower.tail = TRUE, log.p = FALSE)  qgammamix(   p,   w,   shape,   scale,   lower.tail = TRUE,   log.p = FALSE,   tol = 1e-10,   maxiter = 200 )  rgammamix(n, w, shape, scale)  dgammamixgpd(   x,   w,   shape,   scale,   threshold,   tail_scale,   tail_shape,   log = FALSE )  pgammamixgpd(   q,   w,   shape,   scale,   threshold,   tail_scale,   tail_shape,   lower.tail = TRUE,   log.p = FALSE )  qgammamixgpd(   p,   w,   shape,   scale,   threshold,   tail_scale,   tail_shape,   lower.tail = TRUE,   log.p = FALSE,   tol = 1e-10,   maxiter = 200 )  rgammamixgpd(n, w, shape, scale, threshold, tail_scale, tail_shape)  dgammagpd(x, shape, scale, threshold, tail_scale, tail_shape, log = FALSE)  pgammagpd(   q,   shape,   scale,   threshold,   tail_scale,   tail_shape,   lower.tail = TRUE,   log.p = FALSE )  qgammagpd(   p,   shape,   scale,   threshold,   tail_scale,   tail_shape,   lower.tail = TRUE,   log.p = FALSE )  rgammagpd(n, shape, scale, threshold, tail_scale, tail_shape)

title: Lowercase vectorized Gamma distribution functions — gamma_lowercase
text: x Numeric vector quantiles. w Numeric vector mixture weights. shape, scale Numeric vectors (mix) scalars (base+gpd) component parameters. log Logical; TRUE, return log-density. q Numeric vector quantiles. lower.tail Logical; TRUE (default), probabilities \(P(X \le x)\). log.p Logical; TRUE, probabilities log scale. p Numeric vector probabilities. tol, maxiter Tolerance max iterations numerical inversion. n Integer number observations generate. threshold, tail_scale, tail_shape GPD tail parameters (scalars).

title: Lowercase vectorized Gamma distribution functions — gamma_lowercase
text: Numeric vector densities, probabilities, quantiles, random variates.

title: Lowercase vectorized Gamma distribution functions — gamma_lowercase
text: dgammamix(): Gamma mixture density (vectorized) pgammamix(): Gamma mixture distribution function (vectorized) qgammamix(): Gamma mixture quantile function (vectorized) rgammamix(): Gamma mixture random generation (vectorized) dgammamixgpd(): Gamma mixture + GPD density (vectorized) pgammamixgpd(): Gamma mixture + GPD distribution function (vectorized) qgammamixgpd(): Gamma mixture + GPD quantile function (vectorized) rgammamixgpd(): Gamma mixture + GPD random generation (vectorized) dgammagpd(): Gamma + GPD density (vectorized) pgammagpd(): Gamma + GPD distribution function (vectorized) qgammagpd(): Gamma + GPD quantile function (vectorized) rgammagpd(): Gamma + GPD random generation (vectorized)

title: Lowercase vectorized Gamma distribution functions — gamma_lowercase
code: w <- c(0.55, 0.3, 0.15) shp <- c(2, 4, 6) scl <- c(1, 2.5, 5)  # Gamma mixture dgammamix(c(1, 2, 3), w = w, shape = shp, scale = scl) #> Error in dgammamix(c(1, 2, 3), w = w, shape = shp, scale = scl): could not find function "dgammamix" rgammamix(5, w = w, shape = shp, scale = scl) #> Error in rgammamix(5, w = w, shape = shp, scale = scl): could not find function "rgammamix"  # Gamma mixture + GPD dgammamixgpd(c(2, 3, 4), w = w, shape = shp, scale = scl,              threshold = 3, tail_scale = 0.9, tail_shape = 0.2) #> Error in dgammamixgpd(c(2, 3, 4), w = w, shape = shp, scale = scl, threshold = 3,     tail_scale = 0.9, tail_shape = 0.2): could not find function "dgammamixgpd"  # Gamma + GPD (single component) dgammagpd(c(2, 3, 4), shape = 4, scale = 2.5, threshold = 3,           tail_scale = 0.9, tail_shape = 0.2) #> Error in dgammagpd(c(2, 3, 4), shape = 4, scale = 2.5, threshold = 3,     tail_scale = 0.9, tail_shape = 0.2): could not find function "dgammagpd"

title: Gamma mixture distribution — gamma_mix
text: finite mixture Gamma components. Base Gamma functions taken stats. Mixture density CDF computed weighted sums. Random generation samples component according weights draws corresponding component. Quantiles computed numerical inversion mixture CDF. d*, p*, q* functions accept vector inputs first argument evaluate elementwise; r* supports n > 1.

title: Gamma mixture distribution — gamma_mix
code: dGammaMix(x, w, shape, scale, log = 0)  pGammaMix(q, w, shape, scale, lower.tail = 1, log.p = 0)  rGammaMix(n, w, shape, scale)  qGammaMix(   p,   w,   shape,   scale,   lower.tail = TRUE,   log.p = FALSE,   tol = 1e-10,   maxiter = 200 )

title: Gamma mixture distribution — gamma_mix
text: x Numeric scalar giving point density evaluated. w Numeric vector mixture weights length \(K\). functions normalize w internally needed. shape, scale Numeric vectors length \(K\) giving Gamma shapes rates. log Logical; TRUE, return log-density. q Numeric scalar giving point distribution function evaluated. lower.tail Logical; TRUE (default), probabilities \(P(X \le q)\). log.p Logical; TRUE, probabilities returned log scale. n Integer giving number draws. RNG implementation supports n = 1. p Numeric scalar probability \((0,1)\) quantile function. tol Numeric scalar tolerance passed stats::uniroot. maxiter Integer maximum number iterations stats::uniroot.

title: Gamma mixture distribution — gamma_mix
text: Density/CDF/RNG functions return numeric scalars. qGammaMix returns numeric vector length p.

title: Generalized Pareto distribution — gpd
text: Base generalized Pareto distribution (GPD) threshold exceedances threshold. Parameterization uses threshold threshold, scale scale > 0, shape shape. d*, p*, q* functions accept vector inputs first argument evaluate elementwise; r* supports n > 1.

title: Generalized Pareto distribution — gpd
code: dGpd(x, threshold, scale, shape, log = 0)  pGpd(q, threshold, scale, shape, lower.tail = 1, log.p = 0)  rGpd(n, threshold, scale, shape)  qGpd(p, threshold, scale, shape, lower.tail = TRUE, log.p = FALSE)

title: Generalized Pareto distribution — gpd
text: x Numeric scalar giving point density evaluated. threshold Numeric scalar threshold GPD attached. scale Numeric scalar GPD scale parameter; must positive. shape Numeric scalar GPD shape parameter. log Logical; TRUE, return log-density (integer flag 0/1 NIMBLE). q Numeric scalar giving point distribution function evaluated. lower.tail Logical; TRUE (default), probabilities \(P(X \le q)\). log.p Logical; TRUE, probabilities returned log scale. n Integer giving number draws. portability inside NIMBLE, RNG implementation supports n = 1. p Numeric scalar probability \((0,1)\) quantile function.

title: Generalized Pareto distribution — gpd

title: Inverse Gaussian (Wald) distribution — InvGauss
text: inverse Gaussian (also called Wald distribution) positive-support model right-skewed often used waiting times. package provides NIMBLE-compatible density, CDF, RNG functions mean/shape parameterization (mean \(\mu>0\), shape \(\lambda>0\)). standalone mixture quantile function computed numerical inversion elsewhere; qinvGauss inverts base CDF. d*, p*, q* functions accept vector inputs first argument evaluate elementwise; r* supports n > 1.

title: Inverse Gaussian (Wald) distribution — InvGauss
code: dInvGauss(x, mean, shape, log = 0)  pInvGauss(q, mean, shape, lower.tail = 1, log.p = 0)  rInvGauss(n, mean, shape)  qInvGauss(   p,   mean,   shape,   lower.tail = TRUE,   log.p = FALSE,   tol = 1e-10,   maxiter = 200 )

title: Inverse Gaussian (Wald) distribution — InvGauss
text: x Numeric scalar giving point density evaluated. mean Numeric scalar mean parameter \(\mu>0\). shape Numeric scalar shape parameter \(\lambda>0\). log Logical; TRUE, return log-density (integer flag 0/1 NIMBLE). q Numeric scalar giving point distribution function evaluated. lower.tail Logical; TRUE (default), probabilities \(P(X \le q)\). log.p Logical; TRUE, probabilities returned log scale. n Integer giving number draws. portability inside NIMBLE, RNG implementation supports n = 1. p Numeric scalar giving probability quantile. tol Numeric scalar tolerance passed stats::uniroot. maxiter Integer maximum number iterations stats::uniroot.

text: dInvGauss returns numeric scalar density; pInvGauss returns numeric scalar CDF; rInvGauss returns one random draw; qinvGauss returns numeric quantile.

title: Lognormal mixture distribution — lognormal_mix
text: finite mixture Lognormal components. Base Lognormal functions taken stats. Mixture density CDF computed weighted sums. Random generation samples component index according weights draws corresponding component. Quantiles computed numerical inversion mixture CDF. d*, p*, q* functions accept vector inputs first argument evaluate elementwise; r* supports n > 1.

what: Lognormal mixture with a Gpd tail — lognormal_mixgpd
title: Lognormal mixture with a Gpd tail — lognormal_mixgpd
text: Splices generalized Pareto distribution (Gpd) threshold onto Lognormal mixture bulk. Let \(F_{mix}\) Lognormal mixture CDF. spliced CDF \(F(x)=F_{mix}(x)\) \(x<threshold\) \(F(x)=F_{mix}(threshold) + \{1-F_{mix}(threshold)\}G(x)\) \(x\ge threshold\), \(G\) Gpd CDF exceedances threshold.

title: Lognormal mixture with a Gpd tail — lognormal_mixgpd

code: w <- c(0.6, 0.25, 0.15) mu <- c(-1, 0.5, 2) sig <- c(1, 0.7, 1.3)  # Normal mixture dnormmix(c(0, 1, 2), w = w, mean = mu, sd = sig) #> Error in dnormmix(c(0, 1, 2), w = w, mean = mu, sd = sig): could not find function "dnormmix" rnormmix(5, w = w, mean = mu, sd = sig) #> Error in rnormmix(5, w = w, mean = mu, sd = sig): could not find function "rnormmix"  # Normal mixture + GPD dnormmixgpd(c(1, 2, 3), w = w, mean = mu, sd = sig,             threshold = 2, tail_scale = 1, tail_shape = 0.2) #> Error in dnormmixgpd(c(1, 2, 3), w = w, mean = mu, sd = sig, threshold = 2,     tail_scale = 1, tail_shape = 0.2): could not find function "dnormmixgpd"  # Normal + GPD (single component) dnormgpd(c(1, 2, 3), mean = 0.5, sd = 1, threshold = 2,          tail_scale = 1, tail_shape = 0.2) #> Error in dnormgpd(c(1, 2, 3), mean = 0.5, sd = 1, threshold = 2, tail_scale = 1,     tail_shape = 0.2): could not find function "dnormgpd"

title: Normal mixture distribution — normal_mix

Random generation samples component index according weights draws corresponding component. Quantiles computed numerical inversion mixture CDF. d*, p*, q* functions accept vector inputs first argument evaluate elementwise; r* supports n > 1.

title: Normal mixture distribution — normal_mix

RNG implementation supports n = 1. p Numeric scalar probability \((0,1)\) quantile function. tol Numeric scalar tolerance passed stats::uniroot. maxiter Integer maximum number iterations stats::uniroot.

title: Predict from a causal fit — predict.dpmixgpd_causal_fit
text: object "dpmixgpd_causal_fit" object returned run_mcmc_causal(). x Optional new data. Alias newdata. y Numeric vector evaluation points (required type="density" "survival"). ps Optional numeric vector propensity scores aligned x / newdata. provided, supplied scores used instead recomputing stored PS model (needed custom inputs). newdata Optional new data. NULL, uses training design (stored). type Prediction type. Supported: "mean", "quantile", "density", "survival", "prob". p Numeric vector probabilities quantiles (required type="quantile"). nsim Number posterior predictive samples (type="sample"). interval Character NULL; type credible interval: NULL interval, "credible" equal-tailed quantile intervals (default), "hpd" highest posterior density intervals. probs Quantiles credible interval bands. store_draws Logical; whether store posterior draws (type="sample"). nsim_mean Number posterior predictive samples use posterior mean estimation (type="mean"). ncores Number CPU cores use parallel prediction (supported). ... Unused.

title: Predict from a causal fit — predict.dpmixgpd_causal_fit
text: "mean" "quantile", numeric matrix columns ps, estimate, lower, upper, representing treated-minus-control posterior summaries. PS disabled X absent, ps NA PS used. "density", "survival", "prob", data frame columns y, ps, trt_estimate, trt_lower, trt_upper, con_estimate, con_lower, con_upper.

title: Predict from a causal fit — predict.dpmixgpd_causal_fit
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cb <- build_causal_bundle(y = y, X = X, T = T, backend = "sb", kernel = "normal") fit <- run_mcmc_causal(cb) predict(fit, x = X[1:10, ], type = "quantile", p = c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75)) predict(fit, x = X[1:10, ], type = "mean", interval = "hpd")  # HPD intervals predict(fit, x = X[1:10, ], type = "mean", interval = NULL)   # No intervals } # }

title: Predict from a MixGPD fit — predict.mixgpd_fit
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ y <- abs(stats::rnorm(50)) + 0.1 bundle <- build_nimble_bundle(y = y, backend = "sb", kernel = "normal",                              GPD = TRUE, components = 6,                              mcmc = list(niter = 200, nburnin = 50, thin = 1, nchains = 1)) fit <- run_mcmc_bundle_manual(bundle) pr <- predict(fit, type = "quantile", p = c(0.5, 0.9)) pr_surv <- predict(fit, y = sort(y), type = "survival") pr_cdf <- list(fit = 1 - pr_surv$fit) # HPD intervals pr_hpd <- predict(fit, type = "quantile", p = c(0.5, 0.9), interval = "hpd") # No intervals pr_none <- predict(fit, type = "quantile", p = c(0.5, 0.9), interval = NULL) } # }

title: Quantile treatment effects (QTE) — qte
text: Computes treated-minus-control quantiles causal fit.

code: qte(   fit,   probs = c(0.1, 0.5, 0.9),   newdata = NULL,   interval = "credible",   level = 0.95 )

title: Quantile treatment effects (QTE) — qte
text: fit "dpmixgpd_causal_fit" object run_mcmc_causal(). probs Numeric vector probabilities (0, 1) specifying quantile levels outcome distribution estimate treatment effects . newdata Optional data.frame matrix covariates prediction. interval Character NULL; type credible interval: NULL interval, "credible" equal-tailed quantile intervals (default), "hpd" highest posterior density intervals. level Numeric credible level intervals (default 0.95 95 percent CI).

title: Quantile treatment effects (QTE) — qte
text: list elements fit (QTE), grid (probabilities), treated/control prediction objects.

title: Quantile treatment effects (QTE) — qte
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ cb <- build_causal_bundle(y = y, X = X, T = T, backend = "sb", kernel = "normal", J = 6) fit <- run_mcmc_causal(cb, show_progress = FALSE) qte(fit, probs = c(0.5, 0.9), newdata = X[1:5, ]) qte(fit, probs = c(0.5, 0.9), interval = "credible", level = 0.90)  # 90% CI qte(fit, probs = c(0.5, 0.9), interval = "hpd")  # HPD intervals qte(fit, probs = c(0.5, 0.9), interval = NULL)   # No intervals } # }
